message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

groom Bb.  I want to now explore improving our ACG by finding some exemplary bash modules that correspond with robustly healthy RBS*.adoc subdocuments.  I _think_ more recently touched subdocuments might be good candidates for this.  I do know there is a wide range of conformance here, so finding an exemplar to build out ACG is important.  Orient yourself and help me determine how to proceed.

addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Heat: rbk-10-mvp-acg-scrub (₣Bb)
Status: stabled
Created: 260608


## Paces

### acg-v1-author-allocation-guide (₢BbAAA) [rough]

## Character
Doc authoring from settled design — shape fully derived in the seed conversation
(see paddock); transcription, not invention.

Create ACG (Allocation Coding Guide) as a veiled markdown guide. Veiled (a
vov_veiled/ directory), guide-family sibling to BCG/RCG/WSG/CBG; exact kit home
is a mount-time call. Proprietary — not released at MVP. Register the mint: add
the ACG acronym + expansion to the relevant claude-*-context.md acronym map and
the CLAUDE.md guide registry.

Spine: don't recreate inline what has a named home; reference the home. Values ->
constants; concepts -> quoin-refs. MCM builds the homes; ACG owns source's
obligation to reference them rather than recreate.

Cover at sapling depth:
- Three homes by when-read: design-time -> spec; edit-time -> source comment
  (operational mechanics only — language idiom + Pale/foreign-boundary);
  execution-time -> runtime announcement.
- Two source-doc forms: contract header (bounded comment, only where the
  contract is not self-evident from name+body); intent announcement (crisp
  printout on the verb surface). Mechanism is BUC/BCG; ACG owns when+what, not
  the buc_* how.
- Runtime education both paths: happy path announces intent; failure path
  discloses the option space (live exemplar: rbfc_require_vessel_sigil).

Posture: bless and name emergent practices; state the spine. Do NOT mandate
universals (e.g. "every verb must announce") — mark as candidates. No
qualify-enforcement in v1.

### rbrv-env-bash-sweep (₢BbAAE) [rough]

## Character
Mechanical sweep gated on one small design fork at its head — the first worked
application of ACG's magic-string discipline.

## Goal
Give the rbrv.env filename literal a single named home in bash; sweep the
path-construction sites onto it.

## Head fork (settle at mount)
Does the constant reference the existing rbrv_regime quoin (RBS0) or a freshly
minted filename quoin distinct from the regime? The regime-vs-file split is real
— sourcing/validation act on the regime; yoke/inscribe/enumerate act on the file
— so lean to a distinct filename quoin, but confirm against MCM "warrants
anchoring".

## Locked
- Constant joins the RBCC_rbr*_file family in rbcc_Constants.sh (sibling to
  RBCC_rbrn_file / rbra_file / rbro_file), with a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
- Sweep only genuine path constructions. Discover: grep -rn "rbrv\.env" Tools/rbk
  --include="*.sh"; exclude prose — doc_params, buc_info/buh_line messages, and
  comments keep the literal (naming the file for a human is not a magic string).

## Done
Every bash path-construction resolves through the constant; prose literals
untouched; the constant carries its quoin breadcrumb; tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh and
tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh green.

### rbrv-env-rust-sweep (₢BbAAF) [rough]

## Character
Mechanical — RCG Constant Discipline: dedup plus magic-string removal in the
theurge crate (rbtd).

## Goal
The same single-home treatment for rbrv.env in Rust.

## Locked
- Add RBTDGC_RBRV_FILE to the regime-file family in rbtdgc_consts.rs (sibling to
  RBTDGC_RBRN_FILE / RBRA_FILE / RBRO_FILE).
- Collapse the two ad-hoc local consts onto it — discover the `const ... =
  "rbrv.env"` definitions in rbtdro_onboarding.rs and rbtdrp_pristine.rs — and
  replace the bare `path.join("rbrv.env")` magic strings. Comments and
  error-format-strings keep the literal.

## Done
One shared RBTDGC_RBRV_FILE; no local duplicate consts; no bare path-join magic
strings; tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh and tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh green.

### vocab-eviction-mechanism (₢BbAAB) [rough]

## Character
Term-agnostic test infrastructure — build the engine, not the word list.
Self-proving; zero real evicted terms.

## Goal
Stand up the vocabulary-eviction mechanism as a theurge fixture (fast tier, no
deps): a data-driven engine reading rows of {kill_stem, keep_contexts}, scanning
Tools/ and tt/ for each kill_stem with identifier-boundary awareness, asserting
zero hits outside the declared keep_contexts. Ship one self-test row proving both
a caught violation and a respected keep-context — but NO production evicted terms
(population is deferred, per-cluster, behind each term's own cutover).

## Locked
- One engine for all terms: a pure corpse is a row with empty keep_contexts. Do
  not split grep-for-simple / code-for-overloaded.
- Identifier/sprue awareness is the whole reason this is code, not grep —
  keep_contexts must distinguish a kill-stem-as-token from the same stem living
  inside a kept identifier.
- Register in the fast suite. New fixture file; the engine is independent of any
  heat's specific rename.
- Name it in ACG: add the section declaring this fixture THE canonical home for
  evicted-term assertions, with the rule "retire a term -> add a row here, never
  improvise a grep." Mirrors the BUK guide naming bug_require_clean_tree as the
  canonical clean-tree gate.

## Done
Fixture green in fast with its self-test row (one caught violation, one respected
keep-context); ACG precisely names it as the evicted-term home; no production
evicted terms yet.

### noun-cluster-crucible-family (₢BbAAC) [rough]

## Character
Stable-noun curation — terms are BH/BZ-safe so the docket is concrete, but
execution is file-gated on BH.

## Goal
Curate the crucible-runtime noun cluster (Crucible, Bottle, Sentry, Pentacle):
each traces to one canonical home (RBS0 quoin; RBYC fragment where
handbook-surfaced), and its drift-prone uses are made consistent — enrollment
descriptions and doc_briefs agree, no variant spellings, no in-source comment
re-definitions of what the noun means.

## Locked
- Curate, never evict — these nouns live. Removal of dead terms is the ₢BbAAB
  mechanism + BH's vocab scrub, not here.
- Target only drift-prone uses (cross-referencing descriptions, variant
  spellings, comment re-definitions); leave legitimate prose mentions alone.
- Apply ACG's reference-the-home discipline.

## Gate
After ₣BH (lode-universal-capture) lands — these descriptions live in
rbz_zipper.sh and the doc_briefs BH is rewriting.

## Done
Each cluster noun traces to one home; descriptions and doc_briefs consistent; no
variant spellings or in-source re-definitions survive; fast green.

### noun-cluster-vessel-identity (₢BbAAD) [rough]

## Character
Stable-noun curation — terms are BH/BZ-safe so the docket is concrete, but
execution is file-gated on BH.

## Goal
Curate the vessel-identity noun cluster (Vessel, Sigil, Nameplate): each traces
to one canonical home (RBS0 quoin; RBYC fragment where handbook-surfaced), and
its drift-prone uses are made consistent — enrollment descriptions and doc_briefs
agree, no variant spellings, no in-source comment re-definitions of what the noun
means. Watch the Vessel/Sigil boundary specifically (sigil = the per-vessel
directory name; vessel = the configured unit) so the two don't blur in prose.

## Locked
- Curate, never evict — these nouns live. Removal of dead terms is the ₢BbAAB
  mechanism + BH's vocab scrub, not here.
- Target only drift-prone uses (cross-referencing descriptions, variant
  spellings, comment re-definitions); leave legitimate prose mentions alone.
- Apply ACG's reference-the-home discipline.

## Gate
After ₣BH (lode-universal-capture) lands — these descriptions live in
rbz_zipper.sh and the doc_briefs BH is rewriting.

## Done
Each cluster noun traces to one home; descriptions and doc_briefs consistent; no
variant spellings or in-source re-definitions survive; fast green.

content.text: Let me read the full paddock from the gazette.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣Bb
2	
3	## Shape
4	
5	Pre-MVP source/spec hygiene, feathered in pace-at-a-time alongside MVP feature
6	finalization. Each pace carries a targeted blast radius that merges trivially
7	into final feature work — no cross-cutting refactors. A seed, not a tree: scope
8	stays tight to the source/spec allocation family (magic-string -> constant
9	sweeps, decision-detail hoisting, source-documentation standards). Resist
10	becoming the junk drawer for unrelated cleanups.
11	
12	## Governing idea
13	
14	The ACG (Allocation Coding Guide) spine: don't recreate inline what has a named
15	home; reference the home. Values -> constants; concepts -> quoin-refs. MCM builds
16	the named homes; ACG governs source's obligation to reference rather than
17	recreate, and where prose with no spec-home actually goes.
18	
19	Three homes by when-read: design-time -> spec, edit-time -> source comment
20	(operational mechanics only — language idiom + Pale/foreign-boundary),
21	execution-time -> runtime announcement. Most conceptual comments are temporal
22	misallocation — design-time or execution-time knowledge dumped into the
23	edit-time medium, where it rots for lack of a forcing function.
24	
25	## Cinched
26	
27	- ACG = "Allocation Coding Guide" (not "Asciidoc" — the guide governs source
28	  authoring/allocation, barely touches asciidoc; that is MCM's domain).
29	  Veiled/proprietary, guide-family sibling to BCG/RCG/WSG/CBG. Not released at
30	  MVP.
31	- v1 posture: bless and name emergent practices, state the spine. Do NOT mandate
32	  universals ("every verb must announce") — those are candidates, confirmed
33	  pace-by-pace. No qualify-enforcement in v1.
34	- Two source-doc forms are distinct and both blessed: contract header (bounded
35	  comment, edit-time) vs intent announcement (runtime printout, execution-time).
36	  Failure-path option disclosure (the missing-param-shows-options practice, live
37	  in rbfc_require_vessel_sigil) is blessed alongside happy-path announcement.
38	
39	## Sequencing & lanes
40	
41	The ACG guide and the eviction mechanism are heat-internal and buildable now, no
42	cross-heat dependency. The vocabulary work splits by who owns the terms:
43	
44	- Eviction (removing superseded terms) is gated on ₣BH — the lode-capture heat
45	  curates the KILL/KEEP list and retires the terms; the mechanism pace builds the
46	  standing theurge engine, and its rows populate per-cluster behind ₣BH's
47	  cutovers. ACG names that engine as the canonical home for evicted-term
48	  assertions (ACG eating its own dog food — a named home, referenced not
49	  recreated).
50	- Cluster repair (curating living vocabulary to a single home) is disjoint from
51	  eviction and never removes terms. The slated noun clusters are BH/BZ-safe in
52	  their terms but file-gated on ₣BH, since their descriptions share rbz_zipper.sh
53	  and the doc_briefs ₣BH is rewriting.
54	
55	Deliberately not slated until their owning heats land: reliquary / Lode / Hallmark
56	vocabulary (₣BH's lane), Manor / Depot / account-role vocabulary (₣BZ's lane,
57	citizen-model), and verb clusters (₣BH curates its own verb descriptions during
58	cutover). Slating against terms two hot heats are still moving would only
59	re-create the drift this heat exists to kill.
60	
61	## Done looks like
62	
63	ACG v1 exists and is reachable from BCG/RCG; the rbrv.env literal has a single
64	named home in both bash and rust as the first worked application of the
65	magic-string discipline.
66	
67	# jjezs_pace ₢BbAAA
68	
69	## Character
70	Doc authoring from settled design — shape fully derived in the seed conversation
71	(see paddock); transcription, not invention.
72	
73	Create ACG (Allocation Coding Guide) as a veiled markdown guide. Veiled (a
74	vov_veiled/ directory), guide-family sibling to BCG/RCG/WSG/CBG; exact kit home
75	is a mount-time call. Proprietary — not released at MVP. Register the mint: add
76	the ACG acronym + expansion to the relevant claude-*-context.md acronym map and
77	the CLAUDE.md guide registry.
78	
79	Spine: don't recreate inline what has a named home; reference the home. Values ->
80	constants; concepts -> quoin-refs. MCM builds the homes; ACG owns source's
81	obligation to reference them rather than recreate.
82	
83	Cover at sapling depth:
84	- Three homes by when-read: design-time -> spec; edit-time -> source comment
85	  (operational mechanics only — language idiom + Pale/foreign-boundary);
86	  execution-time -> runtime announcement.
87	- Two source-doc forms: contract header (bounded comment, only where the
88	  contract is not self-evident from name+body); intent announcement (crisp
89	  printout on the verb surface). Mechanism is BUC/BCG; ACG owns when+what, not
90	  the buc_* how.
91	- Runtime education both paths: happy path announces intent; failure path
92	  discloses the option space (live exemplar: rbfc_require_vessel_sigil).
93	
94	Posture: bless and name emergent practices; state the spine. Do NOT mandate
95	universals (e.g. "every verb must announce") — mark as candidates. No
96	qualify-enforcement in v1.
97	
98	# jjezs_pace ₢BbAAB

# jjezs_paddock ₣Bb

## Shape

Pre-MVP source/spec hygiene, feathered in pace-at-a-time alongside MVP feature
finalization. Each pace carries a targeted blast radius that merges trivially
into final feature work — no cross-cutting refactors. A seed, not a tree: scope
stays tight to the source/spec allocation family (magic-string -> constant
sweeps, decision-detail hoisting, source-documentation standards). Resist
becoming the junk drawer for unrelated cleanups.

## Governing idea

The ACG (Allocation Coding Guide) spine: don't recreate inline what has a named
home; reference the home. Values -> constants; concepts -> quoin-refs. MCM builds
the named homes; ACG governs source's obligation to reference rather than
recreate, and where prose with no spec-home actually goes.

Three homes by when-read: design-time -> spec, edit-time -> source comment
(operational mechanics only — language idiom + Pale/foreign-boundary),
execution-time -> runtime announcement. Most conceptual comments are temporal
misallocation — design-time or execution-time knowledge dumped into the
edit-time medium, where it rots for lack of a forcing function.

## Cinched

- ACG = "Allocation Coding Guide" (not "Asciidoc" — the guide governs source
  authoring/allocation, barely touches asciidoc; that is MCM's domain).
  Veiled/proprietary, guide-family sibling to BCG/RCG/WSG/CBG. Not released at
  MVP.
- v1 posture: bless and name emergent practices, state the spine. Do NOT mandate
  universals ("every verb must announce") — those are candidates, confirmed
  pace-by-pace. No qualify-enforcement in v1.
- Two source-doc forms are distinct and both blessed: contract header (bounded
  comment, edit-time) vs intent announcement (runtime printout, execution-time).
  Failure-path option disclosure (the missing-param-shows-options practice, live
  in rbfc_require_vessel_sigil) is blessed alongside happy-path announcement.

## Sequencing & lanes

The ACG guide and the eviction mechanism are heat-internal and buildable now, no
cross-heat dependency. The vocabulary work splits by who owns the terms:

- Eviction (removing superseded terms) is gated on ₣BH — the lode-capture heat
  curates the KILL/KEEP list and retires the terms; the mechanism pace builds the
  standing theurge engine, and its rows populate per-cluster behind ₣BH's
  cutovers. ACG names that engine as the canonical home for evicted-term
  assertions (ACG eating its own dog food — a named home, referenced not
  recreated).
- Cluster repair (curating living vocabulary to a single home) is disjoint from
  eviction and never removes terms. The slated noun clusters are BH/BZ-safe in
  their terms but file-gated on ₣BH, since their descriptions share rbz_zipper.sh
  and the doc_briefs ₣BH is rewriting.

Deliberately not slated until their owning heats land: reliquary / Lode / Hallmark
vocabulary (₣BH's lane), Manor / Depot / account-role vocabulary (₣BZ's lane,
citizen-model), and verb clusters (₣BH curates its own verb descriptions during
cutover). Slating against terms two hot heats are still moving would only
re-create the drift this heat exists to kill.

## Done looks like

ACG v1 exists and is reachable from BCG/RCG; the rbrv.env literal has a single
named home in both bash and rust as the first worked application of the
magic-string discipline.

# jjezs_pace ₢BbAAA

## Character
Doc authoring from settled design — shape fully derived in the seed conversation
(see paddock); transcription, not invention.

Create ACG (Allocation Coding Guide) as a veiled markdown guide. Veiled (a
vov_veiled/ directory), guide-family sibling to BCG/RCG/WSG/CBG; exact kit home
is a mount-time call. Proprietary — not released at MVP. Register the mint: add
the ACG acronym + expansion to the relevant claude-*-context.md acronym map and
the CLAUDE.md guide registry.

Spine: don't recreate inline what has a named home; reference the home. Values ->
constants; concepts -> quoin-refs. MCM builds the homes; ACG owns source's
obligation to reference them rather than recreate.

Cover at sapling depth:
- Three homes by when-read: design-time -> spec; edit-time -> source comment
  (operational mechanics only — language idiom + Pale/foreign-boundary);
  execution-time -> runtime announcement.
- Two source-doc forms: contract header (bounded comment, only where the
  contract is not self-evident from name+body); intent announcement (crisp
  printout on the verb surface). Mechanism is BUC/BCG; ACG owns when+what, not
  the buc_* how.
- Runtime education both paths: happy path announces intent; failure path
  discloses the option space (live exemplar: rbfc_require_vessel_sigil).

Posture: bless and name emergent practices; state the spine. Do NOT mandate
universals (e.g. "every verb must announce") — mark as candidates. No
qualify-enforcement in v1.

# jjezs_pace ₢BbAAB

## Character
Term-agnostic test infrastructure — build the engine, not the word list.
Self-proving; zero real evicted terms.

## Goal
Stand up the vocabulary-eviction mechanism as a theurge fixture (fast tier, no
deps): a data-driven engine reading rows of {kill_stem, keep_contexts}, scanning
Tools/ and tt/ for each kill_stem with identifier-boundary awareness, asserting

(population is deferred, per-cluster, behind each term's own cutover).

## Locked

- Identifier/sprue awareness is the whole reason this is code, not grep —
  keep_contexts must distinguish a kill-stem-as-token from the same stem living
  inside a kept identifier.
- Register in the fast suite. New fixture file; the engine is independent of any
  heat's specific rename.
- Name it in ACG: add the section declaring this fixture THE canonical home for
  evicted-term assertions, with the rule "retire a term -> add a row here, never
  improvise a grep." Mirrors the BUK guide naming bug_require_clean_tree as the
  canonical clean-tree gate.

## Done
Fixture green in fast with its self-test row (one caught violation, one respected
keep-context); ACG precisely names it as the evicted-term home; no production
evicted terms yet.

content.text:
Oriented. Here's where things stand and what I'd do.

**Heat state:** ₣Bb (`rbk-10-mvp-acg-scrub`) is stabled. The two rbrv.env sweeps (₢BbAAE bash, ₢BbAAF rust) just landed — the magic-string spine is now proven in code. But the keystone, **₢BbAAA (author the ACG itself), is still rough and unmounted.** Everything else is gated on ₣BH.

**How your ask fits:** finding an exemplar is the natural front-half of ₢BbAAA. The paddock's v1 posture is *"bless and name emergent practices, state the spine"* — you can't bless a practice you haven't located. So grounding the guide in a real, healthy module+spec pair before writing it is exactly right, not a detour.

**On your recency hypothesis** — it holds, with one honest caveat. Last-touched date is a weak proxy for *healthy*: a spec can date to today off a trivial one-line sweep. Two stronger signals are already in hand:
- **Structural rewrite beats date.** The recently *decomposed* families (rbfc, rbfl) were fully re-authored under current discipline — far better ACG tells than a spec that merely got grazed.
- **You already have a named live exemplar.** The paddock cites `rbfc_require_vessel_sigil` as the failure-path option-disclosure model. That points straight at the **rbfc (Foundry Core)** cluster.

One steer: avoid the Lode specs (RBSLA/LB/LD/LE — all dated 06-04/06-08). They're recent but they're ₣BH's *moving* lane; picking an exemplar there repeats the very drift this heat exists to kill.

**Recommended path:** I do a focused conformance audit over a small candidate set — anchored on **rbfc** plus one or two recency-top settled siblings (e.g. the conjure/ark area, RBSAC touched today) — scoring each on the five ACG tells: values→constants, concept→quoin breadcrumbs, contract headers, intent announcements, failure-path disclosure. I come back with 1–2 exemplar **pairs** (bash module ↔ its RBS subdoc) and a short readout of what each does well, so we pick the one to build ACG v1 around.

Alternatives, if you'd rather: **(a)** pure recency-led — audit the whole 06-08/06-04 cluster blind, no anchor; **(b)** skip the audit, take rbfc on the strength of the existing exemplar and start drafting ACG against it.

Want me to run the audit (the recommended path)? I'd lean on an Explore agent for the fan-out and keep the verdict here.

Running the audit now — five parallel agents over the candidate set (rbfc anchor + settled foundry siblings + one non-foundry contrast), each scoring its module↔spec pair on the ACG tells. I'll synthesize the verdict here.

input.description: Audit rbfc Foundry Core for ACG
input.prompt:
You are auditing a bash module cluster as a candidate EXEMPLAR for a coding guide called the ACG (Allocation Coding Guide). Work in repo /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster.

## What the ACG governs (the spine)
"Don't recreate inline what has a named home; reference the home." Values → named constants; concepts → references to their spec definition (a "quoin"). The spec (an RBS*.adoc subdocument under Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/) is the design-time home; the source comment is the edit-time home (operational mechanics ONLY — language idiom + foreign-boundary notes); the runtime announcement is the execution-time home.

## Your assignment
Audit the **rbfc (Foundry Core)** family as an ACG exemplar candidate. Files live in Tools/rbk/ and match `rbfc*.sh` (e.g. rbfc0_FoundryCore.sh, rbfc0_cli.sh, rbfcv_*.sh, rbfcb_*.sh, rbfca_*.sh, rbfcg_*.sh, rbfcp_*.sh). The acronym map in Tools/rbk/claude-rbk-acronyms.md describes them.

This family contains the function **rbfc_require_vessel_sigil**, already cited elsewhere as a live exemplar of failure-path option disclosure — find it, read it, and assess whether it deserves that reputation.

First, identify the corresponding RBS*.adoc spec subdocument(s) this family traces to (look at what the module references in comments, and the acronym map). Note whether a clean module↔spec pairing exists or whether the family is spread across many specs.

## Score these five ACG "tells" for the bash module (0–5 each, with concrete evidence: file:line refs or grep counts)
1. **Values→constants**: literals (filenames, paths, magic strings) resolve through named constants (RBCC_*, family consts) rather than inline. (Run grep for bare string literals to gauge.)
2. **Concept→quoin breadcrumbs**: comments reference spec quoins/anchors (e.g. a breadcrumb naming an RBS doc + anchor) rather than re-defining concepts inline.
3. **Contract headers**: bounded comment headers on functions ONLY where the contract is not self-evident from name+body — i.e. neither over- nor under-commented.

content.text:
I have everything needed. Here is the audit.

## Pairing
**Confirmed.** `rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh` (vouch/about/vouch_gate/batch_vouch) pairs with `RBSAV-ark_vouch.adoc`. One nuance for the synthesizer: RBSAV is an *operation subdocument* — the MCM mapping section and all `[[anchor]]` definition sites live in the parent **`RBS0-SpecTop.adoc`** (mapping at lines 9–578; anchors `[[rbtgo_ark_vouch]]` :1412, `[[rbtga_ark_vouch]]` :2014, `[[rbtr_mason]]` :1697, `[[rbsk_in_pool_preflight]]` :872, `[[rbsk_no_gcloud]]` :849). RBSAV references those quoins as linked terms. This is a two-file spec home (subdoc + top), not a defect — but the ACG should know the design-time home is the RBS0/RBSAV *pair*, not RBSAV alone.

## Five tell scores (bash module)

**1. Values→constants: 5/5.** Every filename/path/magic string resolves through a named constant. Ark basenames: `RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_{IMAGE,ABOUT,VOUCH,ATTEST}` (defined `rbgc_Constants.sh:186-190`), used ~30× instead of literal `"vouch"`/`"about"`. Roots: `RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT`/`RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT` (`rbgl_GarLayout.sh:47-48`). Curl tuning: `RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC`/`_MAX_TIME_SEC` (`rbcc_Constants.sh:184-185`). Temp prefixes hoisted to readonly module constants in `zrbfv_kindle` (`rbfv:44,47,50`). **Only blemishes:** the `4000` dockerfile-substitution guard is an inline literal (`rbfv:244,440,etc.`) with no home in rbgc/rbcc — defensible since it's commented at `:438-439` explaining the 4096-byte GCB limit, but it's the one value the ACG would flag; and `"rbnve_graft"` appears as a string literal in the graft-only jq (`rbfv:347,364`) rather than a constant, though the `case` arms elsewhere are the canonical token home.

**2. Concept→quoin breadcrumbs: 2/5.** This is the weakest tell. **Zero** bash comments name an RBS doc + anchor (`grep RBSAV|rbtgo_ark_vouch Tools/rbk/*.sh` → empty). The bash carries good *operational* comments (e.g. `rbfv:822-829` explaining the load-then-iterate / FD-not-held-open rationale; `:657-661` mode-branch contract) but none point a maintainer back to the spec's design-time home. A reader at `zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit` has no breadcrumb to RBSAV's NOTE at line 28 that governs exactly this combined-build preflight scoping.

**3. Contract headers: 4/5.** Headers appear precisely where the name isn't self-evident and are absent where it is. `zrbfv_vouch_submit` (`:657-661`) documents the three mode branches; `zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit` (`:180-183`) explains the orphan-gap elimination + args. Public verbs use the structured `buc_doc_brief`/`buc_doc_param` mechanism instead of comment headers (`:105-109, :573-576, :808-809`). No gratuitous headers on self-evident helpers. Minor: `zrbfv_about_submit` (`:419`) gets a one-liner where its mode-fanout arguably warrants more.

**4. Intent announcements: 5/5.** 49 runtime announcement calls (`buc_step`/`buc_info`/`buc_success`/`buc_warn`/`buc_link`). Every gate, auth, submission, and completion announces on the verb surface — e.g. `buc_step "Gating on about artifact existence"` (`:604`), `buc_info "About artifact confirmed..."` (`:626`), `buc_success "Vouch complete..."` (`:653`), plus `buc_link` to the Cloud Console build URL (`:797`). Batch vouch announces per-item progress `[${z_idx}/${z_total}]` (`:909`). Note these are genuine runtime prints; `buc_doc_*` is gated to doc-mode only (`zbuc_doc_mode_predicate`, `buc_command.sh:143-145`) so it's the usage surface, not the announcement surface — the module correctly uses both.

**5. Failure-path option disclosure: 3/5.** Mixed. The flagship case is excellent: `rbfv_vouch` with empty vessel enumerates every available vessel before dying (`:578-587`, `rbrv_list_capture` → `buc_step "Available vessels:"` loop → `buc_die`). But the bulk of param-guards are bare bails: `buc_die "Hallmark parameter required"` (`:116,190,590`), `buc_die "rbfv_vouch_gate: vessel required"` (`:69-70`) disclose no option space. The `Unknown vessel mode` dies (`:476,681`) name the offending value but not the valid set `{rbnve_conjure,rbnve_bind,rbnve_graft}`. 63 `buc_die` sites, one discloses options.

## Spec health: RBSAV (+RBS0): 5/5
Proper MCM concept model. Operation steps use linked-term references throughout (`{rbtgo_ark_vouch}`, `{rbtr_mason}`, `{gcb_service}`, `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}`) resolving to RBS0's mapping section with real `[[anchor]]` definition sites. **Non-stale, verified against code:** every helper the spec implies still exists (`zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit`, `zrbfv_vouch_submit`, `zrbfc_assemble_{vouch,about,preflight}_steps`); the `_RBGV_*` substitution table (`:181-214`) matches the jq emission in `zrbfv_vouch_submit` (`:753-769`) exactly; the spec's `RBDC_POOL_AIRGAP`/`-airgap`-suffix claim (`:16`) matches `rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh:61`; the line-28 NOTE precisely scopes preflight to the combined graft build, matching `zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit`'s `zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step` (`:300`) vs. its absence in standalone `zrbfv_vouch_submit`. The three-mode verdict semantics (DSSE/digest-pin/GRAFTED) align with the bash `case` arms. This is a live, referenceable design-time home.

## Overall exemplar verdict
**Strong on four of five spine tells — a near-exemplar with one named gap.** This pair is the best kind of ACG specimen *because it isn't perfect*: it demonstrates values→constants discipline (tell 1) and runtime intent announcement (tell 4) at near-textbook density, and a spec that is a genuinely live, verified-non-stale design-time home — yet it has **almost no concept→quoin breadcrumbs** (tell 2: zero spec-anchor comments in the bash), which makes it the ideal exemplar for the ACG to bless the *strengths* while naming the breadcrumb gap as the concrete "this is what's missing" teaching case. The ACG should specifically name and bless: (a) the kindle-hoisted readonly temp-prefix constants pattern (`rbfv:44-50`) as the canonical "no inline paths" move, and (b) the empty-vessel enumerate-then-die pattern (`rbfv:578-587`) as the exemplar of failure-path option disclosure — then contrast it against the 62 bare param-dies in the same file to make tell 5 vivid.

content.text:
I have complete evidence. Here is the audit.

## ACG Exemplar Audit: rbgv_AccessProbe.sh (NON-FOUNDRY contrast)

### Pairing: CONFIRMED
- `rbgv_jwt_sa_probe` → quoin `rbtgo_access_jwt_probe` → **RBSAJ** (RBS0-SpecTop.adoc:147 maps the quoin to the bash function name; :1543 includes the subdoc)
- `rbgv_payor_oauth_probe` → quoin `rbtgo_access_oauth_probe` → **RBSAO** (RBS0:148, :1090)
- CLI partner: `Tools/rbk/rbgv_cli.sh` (four role tabtargets: check_governor/retriever/director/payor)

### Five Tell Scores (bash module)

**1. Values→constants — 4/5.** Both URL builders resolve their domain/version through named constants: `${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}...` (rbgv:205) and `${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}...` (rbgv:245), all defined in rbgc_Constants.sh:134-161. Depot/repo/RBRA identities resolve through RBDC (rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh:44-55). Retry policy hoisted to kindle constants `ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS`/`_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC` (rbgv:48-49) and forensic temp paths to `ZRBGV_*_FILE` constants (rbgv:40-45). Only the inline literals are the Apache license URL (boilerplate) and the REST *path skeleton* (`/projects/.../locations/.../repositories/.../packages`) — interpolated, not named. Docked one point: the path skeleton is a magic string with no constant home, and the transient-5xx code set `500|502|503|504` is duplicated as inline case labels in three places (rbgv:137, plus the spec mirrors it).

**2. Concept→quoin breadcrumbs — 1/5.** This is the weakest tell. The bash carries **zero** references back to RBSAJ/RBSAO, to the `rbtgo_*` quoins, or to any spec anchor — grep for `RBSAJ|RBSAO|rbtgo_|quoin|RBS0` returns nothing. The contract headers (rbgv:285-296, :357-362) *re-narrate* the propagation-retry concept inline ("Bounded propagation-absorbing retry. First HTTP 200/206 wins; HTTP 403 the IAM-policy-read 'not yet' signature...") — which is exactly the concept RBSAJ:9-11 already defines as its design-time home. This is the canonical ACG anti-pattern: the concept has a named home, and the source re-defines it inline instead of pointing. The one breadcrumb discipline that *does* appear is cross-module coronet refs in the CLI (rbgv_cli.sh:42 "BBAAp", :127 "BBAAS pattern in rbgp_cli.sh") — but those point at heat history, not at the spec.

**3. Contract headers — 4/5.** Headers appear only on the two external verbs (rbgv:285, :357) and on non-obvious internals where behavior isn't self-evident from name+body: the retry helper (rbgv:100-103 documents the buc_die-vs-return contract), the jq extractor (rbgv:154-156 documents the "exactly one line in all paths" guarantee), the probe-once observers (rbgv:173-177 explains why verdict logic lives in the caller). Trivial helpers like `zrbgv_ms_to_sleep_capture` get a one-liner, not a block. Bounded and earned. Lightly docked because the verb headers spend their words re-narrating spec concepts (the tell-2 problem) rather than on operational mechanics.

**4. Intent announcements — 5/5.** Strong. Every verb opens with a `buc_step` announcing intent on the verb surface: rbgv:313 "JWT SA access probe: role=... count=... delay_ms=...", rbgv:376 "Payor OAuth access probe: count=...", per-attempt steps (rbgv:317, :380), and terminal success steps (rbgv:326, :394). CLI wraps with `buc_step` + `buc_success` bookends (rbgv_cli.sh:47-49, :81-86). 9 buc_step/success calls total; the runtime narrates the whole probe loop.

**5. Failure-path option disclosure — 4/5.** A genuine strength and natural fit for an access probe. Unknown role names the full option space ("expected governor | director | retriever", rbgv:95; mirrored rbgv:310). 403-exhaustion failures surface the extracted `.error.message` body summary via `zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture` (rbgv:340-341, :346-347), which itself guarantees a diagnostic line for empty/non-JSON/malformed bodies (rbgv:157-171). curl-network failures point at the captured stderr file (rbgv:131). Docked one point because the bash 401/403 paths are terser than the spec mandates: RBSAO:65-68 specifies "OAuth credentials expired — run {rbtgo_payor_refresh}" as the 401 remedy, but the bash collapses 401|403 into one "access denied (HTTP ${z_code})" message (rbgv:267-268) with **no** mention of `rbw-gPR`/payor_refresh. The spec discloses the recovery action; the implementation drops it.

### Spec Health — 5/5
Both subdocs are proper MCM operation specs. Every quoin they reference (22 checked: `rbtgo_*`, `gar_service`, `rbtr_*` roles, `rbtoe_*` exchanges, `rbtgi_manor`, `rbrr_*_rbra_file`, `rbrd_gcp_region`, `rbrp_payor_project_id`, and the `rbbc_*` clause vocabulary) resolves **exactly once** in the parent RBS0 mapping section — no orphans, no dangling links. They use the shared `axhob_operation`/`axhos_step`/`axhoc_completion` annotation idiom that 54 other RBS* specs use, and the `rbbc_require/fatal/store/call/show` procedure-clause vocabulary (RBS0:473-478) — concept reuse, not re-invention. Non-stale: RBSAJ:11 correctly documents that the propagation budget (60×3000ms) is supplied by the call-site, which matches rbgv_cli.sh:40-48 exactly. RBSAO:69-73 documents the transient-5xx retry matching the bash. Coherent, current, a real design-time home.

### Overall Exemplar Verdict
**A strong exemplar for tells 3/4/5 and spec health, but a deliberately instructive FAILURE on tell 2 (concept→quoin breadcrumbs).** What the ACG should bless and name here: the runtime-announcement discipline (every verb narrates intent + outcome via buc_step/success bookends) and the option-disclosing failure surface (unknown-role errors enumerate the legal set; HTTP failures surface jq-extracted body summaries through a helper that guarantees a diagnostic line in every path). The spec is a model design-time home — fully anchored, idiom-conformant, non-stale.

As the non-foundry contrast it reveals something the foundry candidates likely won't: **a mature, well-specced module can still completely lack the spec-back-reference breadcrumb.** The concept ("propagation-absorbing 403 retry") has a perfect named home in RBSAJ, yet the bash re-narrates it inline in prose contract headers (rbgv:287-291) with no pointer back — the ACG's central "reference the home, don't recreate it" violation, committed at the *concept* layer even while the *value* layer (tell 1) is disciplined. It also exposes a spec/impl drift in the recovery-disclosure direction: RBSAO prescribes the "run payor_refresh" remedy on 401, the implementation drops it (rbgv:267) — i.e., the runtime *under*-discloses relative to its own spec home. That asymmetry (values referenced, concepts re-narrated, recovery-actions lost) is a cleaner teaching specimen than a foundry module would give, because the spec here is good enough that the missing breadcrumb is the only thing standing between this and a textbook pair.

The bash carries **zero** references back to RBSAJ/RBSAO, to the `rbtgo_*` quoins, or to any spec anchor — grep for `RBSAJ|RBSAO|rbtgo_|quoin|RBS0` returns nothing. The contract headers (rbgv:285-296, :357-362) *re-narrate* the propagation-retry concept inline ("Bounded propagation-absorbing retry. First HTTP 200/206 wins; HTTP 403 the IAM-policy-read 'not yet' signature...") — which is exactly the concept RBSAJ:9-11 already defines as its design-time home. This is the canonical ACG anti-pattern: the concept has a named home, and the source re-defines it inline instead of pointing. The one breadcrumb discipline that *does* appear is cross-module coronet refs in the CLI (rbgv_cli.sh:42 "BBAAp", :127 "BBAAS pattern in rbgp_cli.sh") — but those point at heat history, not at the spec.

**3. Contract headers — 4/5.** Headers appear only on the two external verbs (rbgv:285, :357) and on non-obvious internals where behavior isn't self-evident from name+body: the retry helper (rbgv:100-103 documents the buc_die-vs-return contract), the jq extractor (rbgv:154-156 documents the "exactly one line in all paths" guarantee), the probe-once observers (rbgv:173-177 explains why verdict logic lives in the caller). Trivial helpers like `zrbgv_ms_to_sleep_capture` get a one-liner, not a block. Bounded and earned. Lightly docked because the verb headers spend their words re-narrating spec concepts (the tell-2 problem) rather than on operational mechanics.

**4. Intent announcements — 5/5.** Strong. Every verb opens with a `buc_step` announcing intent on the verb surface: rbgv:313 "JWT SA access probe: role=... count=... delay_ms=...", rbgv:376 "Payor OAuth access probe: count=...", per-attempt steps (rbgv:317, :380), and terminal success steps (rbgv:326, :394). CLI wraps with `buc_step` + `buc_success` bookends (rbgv_cli.sh:47-49, :81-86). 9 buc_step/success calls total; the runtime narrates the whole probe loop.

**5. Failure-path option disclosure — 4/5.** A genuine strength and natural fit for an access probe. Unknown role names the full option space ("expected governor | director | retriever", rbgv:95; mirrored rbgv:310). 403-exhaustion failures surface the extracted `.error.message` body summary via `zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture` (rbgv:340-341, :346-347), which itself guarantees a diagnostic line for empty/non-JSON/malformed bodies (rbgv:157-171). curl-network failures point at the captured stderr file (rbgv:131). Docked one point because the bash 401/403 paths are terser than the spec mandates: RBSAO:65-68 specifies "OAuth credentials expired — run {rbtgo_payor_refresh}" as the 401 remedy, but the bash collapses 401|403 into one "access denied (HTTP ${z_code})" message (rbgv:267-268) with **no** mention of `rbw-gPR`/payor_refresh. The spec discloses the recovery action; the implementation drops it.

### Spec Health — 5/5
Both subdocs are proper MCM operation specs. Every quoin they reference (22 checked: `rbtgo_*`, `gar_service`, `rbtr_*` roles, `rbtoe_*` exchanges, `rbtgi_manor`, `rbrr_*_rbra_file`, `rbrd_gcp_region`, `rbrp_payor_project_id`, and the `rbbc_*` clause vocabulary) resolves **exactly once** in the parent RBS0 mapping section — no orphans, no dangling links. They use the shared `axhob_operation`/`axhos_step`/`axhoc_completion` annotation idiom that 54 other RBS* specs use, and the `rbbc_require/fatal/store/call/show` procedure-clause vocabulary (RBS0:473-478) — concept reuse, not re-invention. Non-stale: RBSAJ:11 correctly documents that the propagation budget (60×3000ms) is supplied by the call-site, which matches rbgv_cli.sh:40-48 exactly. RBSAO:69-73 documents the transient-5xx retry matching the bash. Coherent, current, a real design-time home.

### Overall Exemplar Verdict
**A strong exemplar for tells 3/4/5 and spec health, but a deliberately instructive FAILURE on tell 2 (concept→quoin breadcrumbs).** What the ACG should bless and name here: the runtime-announcement discipline (every verb narrates intent + outcome via buc_step/success bookends) and the option-disclosing failure surface (unknown-role errors enumerate the legal set; HTTP failures surface jq-extracted body summaries through a helper that guarantees a diagnostic line in every path). The spec is a model design-time home — fully anchored, idiom-conformant, non-stale.

As the non-foundry contrast it reveals something the foundry candidates likely won't: **a mature, well-specced module can still completely lack the spec-back-reference breadcrumb.** The concept ("propagation-absorbing 403 retry") has a perfect named home in RBSAJ, yet the bash re-narrates it inline in prose contract headers (rbgv:287-291) with no pointer back — the ACG's central "reference the home, don't recreate it" violation, committed at the *concept* layer even while the *value* layer (tell 1) is disciplined. It also exposes a spec/impl drift in the recovery-disclosure direction: RBSAO prescribes the "run payor_refresh" remedy on 401, the implementation drops it (rbgv:267) — i.e., the runtime *under*-discloses relative to its own spec home. That asymmetry (values referenced, concepts re-narrated, recovery-actions lost) is a cleaner teaching specimen than a foundry module would give, because the spec here is good enough that the missing breadcrumb is the only thing standing between this and a textbook pair.

## ACG Exemplar Audit: rbfc (Foundry Core) family

### Pairing — SPREAD, not 1:1
The rbfc family has **no single dedicated RBFC spec**. It is the bash-side implementation of operations scattered across many RBS subdocuments under one umbrella concept model (RBS0-SpecTop.adoc + its `include::`d RBSA*/RBSC*/RBSI* subdocs):
- **rbfcp_Plumb.sh** → RBSAP-ark_plumb.adoc (cleanest pairing in the family — RBSAP is wholly the plumb operation)
- **rbfca_StepAssembly.sh / rbfcb_BuildHost.sh** → RBSCJ-CloudBuildJson.adoc ("Composed-snippet library")
- **rbfcg_GarRest.sh** → RBSCL (tally) + RBSIR (rekon) + RBSAB/RBSAV (about/vouch ark extraction)
- **rbfcv_VesselResolution.sh** → RBSRV-RegimeVessel.adoc (vessel sigil concept)
- **rbfc0_FoundryCore.sh** (kindle constants) → spans all of the above

So this is a **module-cluster ↔ spec-constellation** relationship, not a tidy module↔spec pair. The bash decomposition (vessel/step/host/gar/plumb clusters) does **not** mirror the spec decomposition (per-operation RBSA* docs). A human synthesizer should note: rbfc is a good *tells* exemplar but a **poor "clean pairing" exemplar** — the ACG should probably name this "umbrella family / spec constellation" as a distinct, acceptable shape rather than holding it up as the 1:1 ideal.

### Five tell scores

**1. Values→constants — 4/5**
Persistent values are centralized as `readonly ZRBFC_*` in the kindle: API bases (rbfc0_FoundryCore.sh:53-55), registry endpoints (:64-66), media types (:69), all temp-file paths (:72-104), and poll-policy magic numbers with a rationale block (:127-141). Reliquary tool refs resolve through `RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_*` constants (rbfca:40-45). Deductions: `rbrv.env` appears as a **bare repeated literal 6×** in rbfcv (lines 36, 62, 67, 96 + comments) with no constant; function-local scratch temp files use inline `${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_*_raw.txt` strings (rbfcg:47,65,112) rather than constants (defensible as ephemera, but not pure).

**2. Concept→quoin breadcrumbs — 2/5**
This is the family's **weakest tell**. Only **2 spec breadcrumbs across 1820 lines**: rbfcb_BuildHost.sh:228 (`RBSCJ "Composed-snippet library"`) and rbfcg_GarRest.sh:72 (`See RBSCL / RBSIR.`). Both are excellent when present — they name the doc AND the anchor-equivalent. But the plumb cluster (which has the cleanest spec home, RBSAP) carries **zero** back-references to RBSAP or its quoins (`rbtgo_ark_inspect`, `rbtc_plumb_full`). Concepts like "vessel sigil," "about ark," "vouch ark" are used in prose but never linked to their RBS0 definition sites. The linkage is one-directional: RBS0:124 points *down* to the colophon (`rbfc_plumb`), but the bash doesn't point *back up*.

**3. Contract headers — 4/5**
Well-calibrated. Functions carry bounded headers stating args/returns/side-effects exactly where non-obvious: rbfcg:132-137 (`Returns: 0 if succeeded, 1 if not found. Infrastructure failures fatal via buc_die`), rbfcb:203-205, rbfca:28-31. The standout is rbfcb:223-236 on `zrbfc_expand_includes` — args, in-place semantics, no-op contract, foreign-boundary note, AND crash-fast rationale. Self-evident validators (`zrbfc_sentinel`, rbfc0:146) correctly carry none. Minor: `zrbfc_load_vessel` (rbfcv:87) has no header despite non-trivial sigil/path-match validation logic.

**4. Intent announcements — 4/5**
Strong on the verb surfaces, absent on internals (correct). rbfcp_Plumb.sh narrates the happy path step-by-step: `buc_step "Resolving vessel from vouch ark"` (:40), `buc_info "Resolved hallmark to vessel: ${z_vessel}"` (:44), `buc_step "Authenticating as Retriever"` (:52), `"Fetching about ark from GAR"` (:65). rbfcb:115 announces poll progress, :156 wall-clock timing. Internal `zrbfc_*` capture helpers stay quiet (e.g. rbfcg list functions have 0 buc_info) — appropriate, they're not verb surfaces.

**5. Failure-path option disclosure — 5/5**
The family's strongest tell, embodied by `rbfc_require_vessel_sigil` (see verdict below). The pattern recurs: `zrbfc_resolve_vessel` (rbfcv:73-84) does the identical list-then-die on bad input.

### `rbfc_require_vessel_sigil` verdict — EARNS the reputation

rbfcv_VesselResolution.sh:31-51. It does **not** bail with a bare error; on missing/invalid sigil it enumerates the option space *before* dying, and distinguishes "you gave nothing" from "you gave a wrong thing":

```bash
z_sigils=$(rbrv_list_capture) || buc_die "No vessels found"
buc_step "Available vessels:"
for z_s in ${z_sigils}; do
  buc_bare "        ${z_s}"
done
if test -z "${z_sigil}"; then
  buc_die "Vessel parameter required"
fi
buc_die "Vessel not found: ${z_sigil}"
```

The two-tier die (`"Vessel parameter required"` vs `"Vessel not found: ${z_sigil}"`) after printing the live, system-derived option list is exactly the failure-path-as-education pattern. The contract header (:28-30) even disclaims scope crisply ("Does NOT resolve to a directory — use zrbfc_resolve_vessel"). Deserved exemplar; the ACG should bless+name this pattern (suggest: **"list-before-die"** or **"failure-path option disclosure"**), noting the option list is *captured live* (`rbrv_list_capture`), not a hard-coded enumeration that could go stale.

### Spec health — 5/5
RBS0-SpecTop.adoc is a genuine, healthy MCM concept model:
- Bounded mapping section (`tag::mapping-section[]` … `end::mapping-section[]`, lines 9-578) with **117 quoin attribute refs** of canonical `:rbt..: <<anchor,Display>>` form.
- **357 `[[anchor]]` definition sites**; verified round-trips (e.g. `:rbtc_plumb_full:` at :168 → `[[rbtc_plumb_full]]` def at :2858; `:rbtga_ark:` at :78 → `[[rbtga_ark]]` at :1867; `:rbtgo_ark_inspect:` at :124 → :1518).
- Subdocs (RBSAP) consume linked terms (`{rbtga_ark}`, `{rbtc_plumb_full}`) and carry operation/voicing annotations (`//axhob_operation`, `//axhos_step`, `//axvo_method`).
- **Non-stale / actively curated**: eliminated quoins are tombstoned with rationale (RBS0:2855-2856 `// rbtc_refresh_gcb_pins eliminated — RBRG regime removed…`). This is a real design-time home the bash *could* reference.

### Overall exemplar verdict
**A strong tells exemplar with one conspicuous gap, and a weak "clean pairing" exemplar.** rbfc nails three of the five ACG tells outright — values-as-kindle-constants, calibrated contract headers, verb-surface intent narration — and `rbfc_require_vessel_sigil` is a textbook failure-path-option-disclosure pattern the ACG should bless and name. The fatal weakness for an ACG *exemplar* is tell #2: with a fully healthy MCM spec sitting right there (5/5), the bash almost never breadcrumbs back to it (2 refs in 1820 lines, none in the plumb cluster despite its clean RBSAP home) — so it demonstrates the design-time home *existing* but not the edit-time→design-time *reference* the ACG spine demands. Recommend: use rbfc to bless the list-before-die pattern, the kindle-constant pattern, and the RBSCJ/RBSCL-style "doc + anchor" breadcrumb (rbfcb:228, rbfcg:72) as the *target*, while explicitly citing the plumb cluster's missing RBSAP back-reference as the **counter-example** of what the ACG is meant to fix.

The standout finding on tell #5: the spec (RBSDY line 54-56) **mandates** the exact failure-disclosure shape, and the implementation (rbfly line 96) **fulfills it precisely** — stamp, subtree path, missing tools, and remediation pointing at the inscribe operation. # ACG Exemplar Audit — rbfl (Foundry Ledger) family

## Pairing chosen for deepest audit: `rbfly_Yoke.sh` ↔ `RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc`

`rbfly`/RBSDY is the cleanest pairing in the family — the verb is conceptually rich (validate-once-then-fan-out-write with resumable failure), and the spec is the family's most detailed operation subdoc. Audited the rest of the family for consistency (rbfli/RBSDI, rbflw/RBSIW, rbfld, rbfln/RBSDL).

**Architecture note:** The RBS*.adoc files are *operation-procedure subdocs* `include::`d into the parent concept model `RBS0-SpecTop.adoc`, which holds the mapping section, anchors, and linked-term definitions. Each rbfl verb has a registered operation quoin in RBS0 (`:rbtgo_director_yoke:` → `[[rbtgo_director_yoke]]` def site at line 1257, include at 1266; same for inscribe/wrest/list). Quoins resolve cleanly.

**Family consistency:** High structural uniformity (decomposition-fresh) — identical license header, `set -euo pipefail`, `zrbfl_sentinel` guard, `buc_doc_*` doc block, `${BUZ_FOLIO:-}` folio intake. Quality of the five tells, however, varies by member; `rbfly` and `rbfln` are strongest, `rbflw` and `rbfli` weakest.

## Five tell scores (bash module)

**1. Values→constants — 4/5.** Strong constant density: 24× `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE`, tool/basename names all flow through `RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_*` / `RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_*`, URLs through `ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE`. Three real inline literals dock it: `gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker` (rbfli_Inscribe.sh:43, also duplicated in rbgp_Payor.sh:718 — a genuine missing constant), `pageSize=1000` (rbfly:46, rbfld:109), and HTTP `"202"`/`"204"` magic numbers (rbfld:76, no constant home in rbgc_Constants.sh). Note `pageSize=1000` is inline project-wide (also rbfcg, rbldl) — not an rbfl-specific lapse.

**2. Concept→quoin breadcrumbs — 1/5 (family's weakest tell).** Zero spec breadcrumbs in any rbfl file: `grep -E 'RBSD[IYLU]|RBSIW|rbtgo_'` over all seven files returns nothing. The convention demonstrably exists in the codebase — 7 sibling rbk files carry it (e.g., `rblds_Spine.sh:31` "Contract: RBSCJ", `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:421` "Spec: RBSAC step..."). The rbfl family, despite each verb having a clean named operation quoin in RBS0, never points back to it. This is the single biggest gap between the family and ACG ideal.

message.content: I want to discuss the precedent we are setting here... Now that you have a framing, help me think through healthy framing.  I am aware I have allowed prior chats to lose detail in treatment here.  How confident are you you have a handle on robustly healthy specification prose?  I generally think specs need extremely crisp and terse prose sequence of steps using AXLA voicings very well to really tie down the sequences involved tersely: maybe some document sections retaining critical tradeoffs and references, but largely lightweight spec framing that allows conceptual operation on lots of sequences/routines/procedures at once without coding considerations, then very crisp and maintainable source but without noisy tradeoff talk.  I think spec needs MCM/AXLA formality at calling conventions, very very language independent type model possible but not required at every place.  Thoughts?

content.text:
Let me answer the confidence question straight first, because it bears on everything else.

**Where I actually stand.** I'm on solid ground on the *formal/structural* layer — what MCM/AXLA prescribe (quoins, motifs, voicings, the `axhob_operation`/`axhos_step` step idiom, the `rbbc_*` clause vocabulary) and on the three-homes logic the ACG is built on. I'm on much softer ground on the precise thing you're asking about: **prose texture** — what *crisp, terse, well-voiced* step prose actually reads like at its best. The reason is concrete: in this session I have not read MCM, AXLA, or a full RBS subdocument myself. My audit ran through agents, and — this is the part that matters for precedent — their spec-health 5/5 scores measured *structural* health: mapping section present, anchors resolve, non-stale, currency. **They did not score prose crispness at all.** So "RBSDY 5/5" is not yet evidence its *prose* is exemplary; it's evidence its skeleton is intact. Structure and prose are different axes, and we've only measured one.

That gap lines up exactly with your own flag that prior chats let detail slip. If the *treatment* drifted, it drifted in the prose layer, which is precisely the layer the audit was blind to. So I'd resist reverse-engineering the ideal from the current specs — they may carry the drift. The ideal has to be triangulated from MCM/AXLA *intent* plus the best-preserved exemplar prose, cross-checked, not "copy the newest spec."

**On your framing — I think it's sound, and it sharpens rather than competes with the ACG's three-homes model.** What you described maps cleanly: spec owns sequence + calling-convention formality + retained tradeoffs (design-time); source owns crisp mechanics with no tradeoff noise (edit-time). The single best thing in it is "tradeoffs live in spec, not source" — that *is* the resolution to the anti-pattern the audit kept hitting (a contract header re-narrating a concept the spec already defines). Tradeoff talk in source is temporal misallocation; moving it to spec gives it a forcing function. I'd bless that without hesitation.

Three places I'd put pressure before it becomes precedent:

1. **Terseness is safe only to the degree a voicing carries the line's semantic load.** A line voiced `axhos_step` or `axk_premise` can be terse because the motif *says what kind of thing it is*. A load-bearing line with no motif for its role becomes cryptic when you compress it — and that's not a license to be terse, it's a signal AXLA is missing a motif. So the rule isn't "terse"; it's "terse where voiced, and unvoiced-but-load-bearing → grow the lexicon." That keeps terseness from going lossy.

2. **"Type model formal in some places, not others" needs a stated rule for *where*, or it reintroduces the exact wide-range-of-conformance drift we're trying to kill.** Your instinct — formality *at calling conventions*, relax internally — is a good rule, if it's *the* rule. The danger isn't selective formality; it's *ad hoc* selective formality, where a future reader can't tell intentional-informality from rot. Name the boundary explicitly and it's clean.

3. **Retained tradeoffs vs. the scannable sequence are in tension** — interleave them and you lose the "operate on many sequences at once" surface that's the whole point. I'd segregate: tradeoffs/rationale as NOTE-style blocks hung off the relevant step, never inline in the step prose. MCM likely already has a home for this (the audit saw `NOTE` blocks like RBSAV:28) — worth confirming the spec already gives this a place rather than inventing one.

So: your framing is right, and I'd be overreaching to claim I can certify it against the real exemplars yet. The honest next move before we set precedent is for me to actually read the texts — MCM (what it says about step prose, voicings, and the type model), AXLA (the voicing vocabulary), and RBSDY's *prose* specifically — and come back with an earned verdict on whether it's a prose exemplar or merely a structural one.

Do you want me to go do that read now, or keep shaping the framing in the abstract a bit more first?

message.content: Do proceed with your read now.  Know that my intent is to build lexers/linters that help us maintain sophisticated voicing constraints in the middle future, so that we can cheaply maintain; I do intend to 'retrofit' RBS*adoc accordingly after its MVP release.  It is in scope to recommend MCM and AXLA nudges that help us move towards that.  I just find myself with some capacity to improve the code while we build out the final MVP features.

content.text:
Reading now — and I'll read the prose myself rather than delegate, since the whole point is to replace inherited confidence with earned. I'll fold in your linter/retrofit intent: as I read, I'm also tracking which voicing constraints are *mechanically checkable as written* versus where MCM/AXLA grammar is loose enough to defeat a future lexer.

Let me size the targets and locate the key anchors first.

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_director_yoke}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation writes `RBRV_RELIQUARY=«STAMP»` into every vessel's {rbrv_regime} file under `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}` so that subsequent Cloud Build operations (conjure, bind, graft, enshrine) consume the named tool image set as GCB step images.
5	The about/vouch pipeline (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft) consumes reliquary tool images uniformly across all ordain modes; bind additionally uses skopeo for image-copy.
6	The stamp is validated once against GAR before any vessel file is touched: all six tool packages must be present under the reliquary subtree.
7	On stamp validation failure the operation errors diagnostically without writing any vessel.
8	On per-vessel write failure the diagnostic identifies the failing vessel and the set of vessels already written, so the operator can resume from a known point.
9	This primitive does not commit; the caller (orchestrator or operator) commits the {rbrv_regime} changes.
10	
11	The
12	{rbtr_director}
13	yokes a reliquary stamp across every vessel:

17	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
18	.. {rbbc_require}
19	`«STAMP»` provided as first positional argument (e.g., `r260327172456`)

24	.. Execute

29	//axhos_step
30	. *List Reliquary Packages in GAR*:
31	.. {rbbc_store}
32	`«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»` as `${RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER}` (resolved by `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`)
33	.. {rbbc_store}
34	`«GAR_REPOSITORY»` as `${RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX}${RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER}-gar` (resolved by `RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY`)
35	.. {rbbc_call}
36	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/list[packages.list] for
37	`«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
38	/
39	{rbrd_gcp_region}
40	/
41	`«GAR_REPOSITORY»`
42	with `pageSize=1000`
43	.. {rbbc_require}
44	HTTP 200 response
45	.. Filter response package names to those starting with `{gar_reliquaries_namespace}/«STAMP»/`
46	.. Strip the subtree prefix to yield the bare tool-name set present in GAR
47	
48	//axhos_step
49	. *Verify Tool Image Completeness*:
50	.. Expected tool name set: `gcloud`, `docker`, `alpine`, `syft`, `binfmt`, `skopeo` (per
51	{rbtgo_depot_inscribe})
52	.. {rbbc_require}
53	all six names present in the filtered list
54	.. {rbbc_fatal}
55	if any expected tool is missing — the error message names `«STAMP»`, the reliquary subtree path, and the specific missing tool(s); remediation points at
56	{rbtgo_depot_inscribe}
57	
58	//axhos_step
59	. *Iterate Vessels under `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}`*:
60	.. Enumerate every immediate subdirectory of `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}` containing an {rbrv_regime} file
61	.. {rbbc_require}
62	at least one vessel found — empty enumeration is a fatal error (nothing to yoke)
63	.. For each enumerated vessel, perform *Rewrite the {rbrv_regime}* below in directory order
64	.. Maintain a running list of vessels successfully written; emit per-vessel progress
65	
66	//axhos_step
67	. *Rewrite the {rbrv_regime}* (per vessel):
68	.. Load the vessel's {rbrv_regime} file into memory, line by line
69	.. For each line matching `^RBRV_RELIQUARY=` — substitute with `RBRV_RELIQUARY=«STAMP»`, preserving position
70	.. All other lines pass through unchanged
71	.. If no `RBRV_RELIQUARY=` line was found — append one under a `# Tool Image Reliquary` comment header
72	.. Write the composed content to a per-vessel temp file, then atomically replace the {rbrv_regime} file via rename
73	.. {rbbc_fatal}
74	on any per-vessel I/O failure — the error message names the failing vessel and the list of vessels already written, so the operator can resume from a known point
75	
76	//axhoc_completion
77	Successful execution writes `RBRV_RELIQUARY=«STAMP»` into the {rbrv_regime} file of every vessel discovered under `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}`; no GAR state is modified.
78	The primitive does not commit — the caller (orchestrator or operator) commits the modified {rbrv_regime} files with their usual git workflow.
79	The reliquary tool images are now linked, but vessel images must be rebuilt
80	({rbtgo_ark_ordain})
81	to pick up the new tool versions.
82

3952	
3953	== Control Voicings
3954	
3955	=== Bash Console Control Voicings
3956	
3957	Control terms for Recipe Bottle operations executing in a bash shell with human operator present.
3958	Each term voices the corresponding
3959	{xref_AXLA}
3960	control motif.
3961	
3962	[[rbbc_variable]]
3963	//axl_voices axc_variable
3964	{rbbc_variable}::
3965	A shell variable denoted by guillemets `«NAME»` storing intermediate results.
3966	Implemented as bash variables or associative array entries.
3967	
3968	[[rbbc_fatal]]
3969	//axl_voices axc_fatal
3970	{rbbc_fatal}::
3971	Red console text followed by `exit 1`.
3972	Script terminates immediately. Human operator must intervene.
3973	
3974	[[rbbc_warn]]
3975	//axl_voices axc_warn
3976	{rbbc_warn}::
3977	Yellow console text indicating non-blocking concern.
3978	Script continues execution. Human operator may note for later.
3979	
3980	[[rbbc_store]]
3981	//axl_voices axc_store
3982	{rbbc_store}::
3983	Capture of a value from command output or API response into a
3984	{rbbc_variable}.
3985	Typically via bash assignment, jq extraction, or Python JSON parsing.
3986	
3987	[[rbbc_use]]
3988	//axl_voices axc_use
3989	{rbbc_use}::
3990	Reference to a previously stored
3991	{rbbc_variable}
3992	value.
3993	Expanded via bash variable substitution.
3994	
3995	[[rbbc_require]]
3996	//axl_voices axc_require
3997	{rbbc_require}::
3998	Validation check that confirms an expected condition.
3999	Triggers
4000	{rbbc_fatal}
4001	if condition not met.
4002	
4003	[[rbbc_poll]]
4004	//axl_voices axc_poll
4005	{rbbc_poll}::
4006	Repeated execution of a command at defined intervals until condition satisfied or timeout.
4007	Implemented as bash while loop with sleep.
4008	
4009	[[rbbc_call]]
4010	//axl_voices axc_call
4011	{rbbc_call}::
4012	Synchronous invocation of a REST API endpoint via curl.
4013	Captures HTTP status code and response body. Returns when response received.
4014	Retries up to 3 times on transient transport errors (connection refused, timeout, SSL, recv failure) with a short sleep between attempts.
4015	
4016	[[rbbc_submit]]
4017	//axl_voices axc_submit
4018	{rbbc_submit}::
4019	Asynchronous invocation of a REST API that returns an LRO operation handle.
4020	Returns immediately with operation ID for later
4021	{rbbc_await}.
4022	
4023	[[rbbc_await]]
4024	//axl_voices axc_await
4025	{rbbc_await}::
4026	Blocking wait on a submitted LRO operation until completion.
4027	Polls operation endpoint until done=true, then returns result.
4028	
4029	[[rbbc_show]]
4030	//axl_voices axc_show
4031	{rbbc_show}::
4032	Presentation of operational facts to the console.
4033	Formatted output via echo or printf to stdout.
4034	
4035	[[rbbc_prompt]]
4036	//axl_voices axc_prompt
4037	{rbbc_prompt}::
4038	Interactive request for human operator confirmation before proceeding.
4039	Displays message and waits for user input (typically y/N).
4040	Script pauses until confirmation received; continues on affirmative, triggers
4041	{rbbc_fatal}
4042	on decline or timeout.
4043	
4044	=== Human Guide Control Voicings
4045	
4046	Control terms for Recipe Bottle procedures executed by a human operator following a rendered document while interacting with external systems (web console, GUI).
4047	Each term voices the corresponding
4048	{xref_AXLA}
4049	control motif.
4050	
4051	[[rbhg_fatal]]
4052	//axl_voices axc_fatal
4053	{rbhg_fatal}::
4054	Instruction to abort the procedure immediately.
4055	Human must not proceed. May require rollback or support contact.
4056	
4057	[[rbhg_warn]]
4058	//axl_voices axc_warn
4059	{rbhg_warn}::
4060	Cautionary note requiring human acknowledgment before proceeding.
4061	Highlights potential issues or irreversible actions.
4062	
4063	[[rbhg_store]]
4064	//axl_voices axc_store
4065	{rbhg_store}::
4066	Instruction for human to record a value for later use.
4067	Typically written down, copied to clipboard, or saved to a file.
4068	
4069	[[rbhg_require]]
4070	//axl_voices axc_require
4071	{rbhg_require}::
4072	Verification checkpoint where human confirms a condition before proceeding.
4073	May involve visual inspection or testing in external UI.
4074	
4075	[[rbhg_show]]
4076	//axl_voices axc_show
4077	{rbhg_show}::
4078	Informational note presented to the human during procedure.
4079	Provides context or expected outcomes without requiring action.

GCP billing account identifier.
Format: `XXXXXX-XXXXXX-XXXXXX`.

== Control Voicings

=== Bash Console Control Voicings

Control terms for Recipe Bottle operations executing in a bash shell with human operator present.
Each term voices the corresponding
{xref_AXLA}
control motif.

[[rbbc_variable]]
//axl_voices axc_variable
{rbbc_variable}::
A shell variable denoted by guillemets `«NAME»` storing intermediate results.
Implemented as bash variables or associative array entries.

[[rbbc_fatal]]
//axl_voices axc_fatal
{rbbc_fatal}::
Red console text followed by `exit 1`.
Script terminates immediately. Human operator must intervene.

[[rbbc_warn]]
//axl_voices axc_warn
{rbbc_warn}::
Yellow console text indicating non-blocking concern.
Script continues execution. Human operator may note for later.

[[rbbc_store]]
//axl_voices axc_store
{rbbc_store}::
Capture of a value from command output or API response into a
{rbbc_variable}.
Typically via bash assignment, jq extraction, or Python JSON parsing.

[[rbbc_use]]
//axl_voices axc_use
{rbbc_use}::
Reference to a previously stored
{rbbc_variable}
value.
Expanded via bash variable substitution.

[[rbbc_require]]
//axl_voices axc_require
{rbbc_require}::
Validation check that confirms an expected condition.
Triggers
{rbbc_fatal}
if condition not met.

[[rbbc_poll]]
//axl_voices axc_poll
{rbbc_poll}::
Repeated execution of a command at defined intervals until condition satisfied or timeout.
Implemented as bash while loop with sleep.

[[rbbc_call]]
//axl_voices axc_call
{rbbc_call}::
Synchronous invocation of a REST API endpoint via curl.
Captures HTTP status code and response body. Returns when response received.
Retries up to 3 times on transient transport errors (connection refused, timeout, SSL, recv failure) with a short sleep between attempts.

[[rbbc_submit]]
//axl_voices axc_submit
{rbbc_submit}::
Asynchronous invocation of a REST API that returns an LRO operation handle.
Returns immediately with operation ID for later
{rbbc_await}.

[[rbbc_await]]
//axl_voices axc_await
{rbbc_await}::
Blocking wait on a submitted LRO operation until completion.
Polls operation endpoint until done=true, then returns result.

[[rbbc_show]]
//axl_voices axc_show
{rbbc_show}::
Presentation of operational facts to the console.
Formatted output via echo or printf to stdout.

[[rbbc_prompt]]
//axl_voices axc_prompt
{rbbc_prompt}::
Interactive request for human operator confirmation before proceeding.
Displays message and waits for user input (typically y/N).
Script pauses until confirmation received; continues on affirmative, triggers
{rbbc_fatal}
on decline or timeout.

=== Human Guide Control Voicings

Control terms for Recipe Bottle procedures executed by a human operator following a rendered document while interacting with external systems (web console, GUI).
Each term voices the corresponding
{xref_AXLA}
control motif.

[[rbhg_fatal]]
//axl_voices axc_fatal
{rbhg_fatal}::
Instruction to abort the procedure immediately.
Human must not proceed. May require rollback or support contact.

[[rbhg_warn]]
//axl_voices axc_warn
{rbhg_warn}::
Cautionary note requiring human acknowledgment before proceeding.
Highlights potential issues or irreversible actions.

[[rbhg_store]]
//axl_voices axc_store
{rbhg_store}::
Instruction for human to record a value for later use.
Typically written down, copied to clipboard, or saved to a file.

[[rbhg_require]]
//axl_voices axc_require
{rbhg_require}::
Verification checkpoint where human confirms a condition before proceeding.
May involve visual inspection or testing in external UI.

[[rbhg_show]]
//axl_voices axc_show
{rbhg_show}::
Informational note presented to the human during procedure.
Provides context or expected outcomes without requiring action.

lastPrompt: Do proceed with your read now.  Know that my intent is to build lexers/linters that help us maintain sophisticated voicing constraints in the middle future, so that we can cheaply maintain; I do inten…

content.text:
RBSDY is a revelation in a good way — let me note what I'm seeing before I finish with MCM and AXLA.

**RBSDY already substantially realizes your framing.** The shape is: `//axhob_operation` → a `NOTE` block carrying *exactly* the segregated tradeoffs/invariants ("validated once before any vessel is touched"; "does not commit; the caller commits") → then a terse numbered step-sequence where each step is headed by a structural voicing (`//axhos_step`, `//axhopt_typed_parameter`, `//axhoc_completion`) and its sub-bullets voice the calling-convention vocabulary (`{rbbc_require}`, `{rbbc_store}`, `{rbbc_call}`, `{rbbc_fatal}`). The `rbbc_*` control terms (RBS0:3962+) are MCM/AXLA formality landing precisely *at the calling conventions*, each one `//axl_voices axc_*` — your instinct, already built. And the RBS0 operation catalog (1257+) is uniform enough to scan dozens of operations at once. This is prose-healthy, not just structurally healthy.

Now MCM (the governing prescriptions) and AXLA (the motif vocabulary) — these are where your linter/retrofit nudges have to come from.
