85	== Core Structure
86	
87	A
88	{mcm_concept_model}
89	creates a cohesive gestalt - a unified constellation of ideas whose relationships and interactions transcend their individual definitions.
90	Through carefully curated terminology and precise linking patterns, each
91	{mcm_concept_model}
92	illuminates the deep conceptual relationships that give meaning to a domain.
93	
94	The power of a
95	{mcm_concept_model}
96	lies in its ability to:
97	
98	* Reveal the natural connections between ideas
99	* Preserve the integrity of concept relationships through evolution
100	* Enable clear visualization of how changes to one concept ripple through the constellation
101	* Guide understanding through carefully mapped idea spaces
102	
103	Each
104	{mcm_concept_model}
105	manifests through a
106	{mcm_document}
107	that follows strict organizational patterns.
108	These patterns ensure that as the constellation evolves, their relationships remain clear and traceable.
109	
110	The
111	{mcm_document}
112	contains two primary constrained element types:
113	
114	* A
115	{mcm_mapping_section}
116	that defines all
117	{mcm_linked_term_s}
118	* One or more
119	{mcm_definition_section_s}
120	that provide precise meaning for each term
121	
122	Through these elements, the
123	{mcm_document}
124	maintains the delicate balance between rigorous structure and organic concept evolution, allowing the idea constellation to grow while preserving its essential relationships.
125	
126	== Text Organization
127	
128	Text within a
129	{mcm_document}
130	follows specific structural rules to enable clear tracking of changes and relationships.
131	
132	=== Line Break Fundamentals
133	
134	The most fundamental organizing principle is the
135	{mcm_line_break}
136	.
137	Each
138	{mcm_line_break}
139	creates a clear boundary in the
140	{mcm_document}
141	while preserving fluid rendering in the output document.
142	
143	==== Basic Line Break Rules
144	
145	{mcm_line_break_s}
146	occur in these precise patterns:
147	
148	* Between independent sentences
149	* Before and after each
150	{mcm_linked_term}
151	(including each term within a sequence)
152	* Between fragments that surround
153	{mcm_linked_term_s}
154	
155	Basic example showing sentence and term breaks:
156	
157	[source,asciidoc]
158	----
159	The system initializes its components.
160	The
161	{excm_processor}
162	begins operation.
163	Each component reports its status.
164	----
165	
166	==== Term Integration Patterns
167	
168	When incorporating
169	{mcm_linked_term_s}
170	the text follows these specific patterns:
171	
172	Single term integration - breaks before and after the term:
173	
174	[source,asciidoc]
175	----
176	The system processes
177	{excm_request}
178	according to defined rules.
179	----
180	
181	Multiple
182	{mcm_linked_term_s}
183	in sequence - each term gets its own line, with commas attached:
184	
185	[source,asciidoc]
186	----
187	The interface supports
188	{excm_drag},
189	{excm_drop},
190	and
191	{excm_scroll}
192	operations.
193	----
194	
195	Complex
196	{mcm_linked_term}
197	relationships - each standalone term gets breaks:
198	
199	[source,asciidoc]
200	----
201	The
202	{excm_validator}
203	applies each
204	{excm_rule}
205	to ensure
206	{excm_compliance}
207	with system requirements.
208	----
209	
210	These patterns apply consistently across all document contexts, including:
211	
212	* Regular paragraphs
213	* List items
214	* Definition entries
215	* Section introductions
216	
217	=== Paragraph Organization
218	
219	Paragraphs in the
220	{mcm_document}
221	follow these structural principles:
222	
223	* {mcm_blank_line_s}
224	appear only between true paragraphs
225	* No
226	{mcm_blank_line_s}
227	between sentences within a paragraph
228	* No
229	{mcm_blank_line_s}
230	around source code blocks or list items
231	* Each paragraph focuses on a single concept or relationship
232	
233	Example paragraph structure:
234	
235	[source,asciidoc]
236	----
237	The system initializes its core components.
238	Each component performs self-diagnostics.
239	The
240	{excm_validator}
241	confirms system health.
242	
243	The initialization process then moves to external systems.
244	The
245	{excm_connector}
246	establishes required links.
247	Each connection undergoes verification.
248	----
249	
250	=== Special Cases
251	
252	==== List Items
253	
254	The
255	{mcm_document}
256	presents list items
257	with proper term integration
258	and conceptual breaks:
259	
260	[source,asciidoc]
261	----
262	* The first item spans
263	{excm_component}
264	boundaries
265	* A second item demonstrates
266	{excm_process}
267	flow
268	* The final item shows
269	{excm_result}
270	formatting
271	----
272	
273	==== Definition Entries
274	
275	Definition entries follow standard sentence breaks while maintaining definition list structure:
276	
277	The single space following `::` is necessary.
278	
279	[source,asciidoc]
280	----
281	[[excm_term]]
282	{excm_example}:: 
283	The primary definition establishes core meaning.
284	Additional sentences expand understanding.
285	The
286	{excm_linked_term}
287	shows relationship handling.
288	----
289	
290	==== Source Code Boundaries
291	
292	Source code blocks establish strict boundaries for
293	{mcm_curation_action_s}.
294	These boundaries protect code content while maintaining proper term integration in surrounding text:
295	
296	[source,asciidoc]
297	----
298	The
299	{excm_processor}
300	implements this interface:
301	
302	[source,java]
303	-----
304	public interface Processor {
305	    void process();
306	    Status check();
307	}
308	-----
309	
310	The implementation ensures
311	{excm_compliance}
312	with system requirements.
313	----
314	
315	{mcm_curation_action_s}
316	respect these boundaries by:
317	
318	* Maintaining term integration and line breaks in surrounding text
319	* Treating code block contents as opaque
320	* Preserving code formatting without modification
321	* Excluding code content from term matching and promotion analysis
322	
323	This boundary pattern ensures code examples remain stable through document evolution while maintaining proper conceptual integration in their context.
324	
325	=== Punctuation Rules
326	
327	Punctuation follows standard English conventions regardless of line breaks:
328	
329	* Sentence-ending punctuation stays with the sentence
330	* Commas remain with their preceding phrase
331	* Quotation marks enclose their content without breaks
332	* Parentheses maintain content unity
333	
334	Example punctuation handling:
335	
336	[source,asciidoc]
337	----
338	The system (version 2.0) activates its components.
339	The
340	{excm_processor}
341	begins its "primary sequence."
342	After initialization,
343	the system reports "ready."
344	----
345	
346	=== Document Mechanics
347	
348	=== {mcm_mapping_section} Constraints
349	
350	The
351	{mcm_mapping_section}
352	enforces alignment rules to enhance readability and change tracking:
353	
354	* The `<<` of each
355	{mcm_domain}
356	aligns to columns that are multiples of 10
357	* When entries require wider alignment, all lines adjust to the next larger multiple
358	* {mcm_attribute_reference_s}
359	maintain consistent indentation
360	
361	[source,asciidoc]
362	----
363	// tag::mapping-example[]
364	:excm_doc_short:              <<excm_short,Short Term>>
365	:excm_doc_longer_term:        <<excm_longer,Longer Term>>
366	:excm_doc_much_longer_term:   <<excm_much_longer,Much Longer Term>>
367	// end::mapping-example[]
368	----
369	
370	The
371	{mcm_mapping_scrub}
372	maintains these alignments during curation unless expansion is required.
373	
374	=== Document Source Management
375	
376	The
377	{mcm_document}
378	maintains compatibility with line-based tools
379	while preserving semantic structure.
380	
381	This means:
382	
383	* Favoring AsciiDoc's native list markers
384	over hardcoded sequence numbers
385	
386	* Preserving processor directives
387	that enable clean rendering
388	
389	* Ensuring changes remain visible
390	in both source differencing tools
391	and rendered comparisons
392	
393	The goal is maintaining a document that:
394	
395	* Shows ancestry clearly in source
396	* Renders cleanly through AsciiDoc
397	* Displays changes meaningfully in diffs
398	* Supports post-rendering analysis
399	
400	=== Term Hierarchy Management
401	
402	When
403	{mcm_linked_term_s}
404	appear to form hierarchical relationships, the
405	{mcm_document}
406	flattens this hierarchy through specific patterns:
407	
408	* Each
409	{mcm_linked_term_s}
410	receives first-class presentation in the
411	{mcm_mapping_section}
412	* Related
413	{mcm_linked_term_s}
414	maintain separate definitions
415	* Relationships express through careful definition crafting
416	
417	Example of hierarchical
418	{mcm_linked_term_s}
419	handling:
420	
421	[source,asciidoc]
422	----
423	:excm_doc_validation:             <<excm_validation,Validation>>
424	:excm_doc_validation_rule:        <<excm_validation_rule,Validation Rule>>
425	:excm_doc_validation_set:         <<excm_validation_set,Validation Set>>
426	----
427	
428	The definitions then express relationships:
429	
430	[source,asciidoc]
431	----
432	[[excm_validation]]
433	{excm_doc_validation}:: 
434	The process of applying rules to ensure content correctness.
435	
436	[[excm_validation_rule]]
437	{excm_doc_validation_rule}:: 
438	A specific constraint within the {excm_doc_validation} framework.
439	
440	[[excm_validation_set]]
441	{excm_doc_validation_set}:: 
442	A collection of {excm_doc_validation_rule_s} that work together.
443	----
444	
445	== Definition Patterns
446	
447	=== Definition Form Patterns
448	
449	Each
450	{mcm_linked_term}
451	in a
452	{mcm_document}
453	requires an anchor target and a definition.
454	The form determines how these elements are structured.
455	
456	[[mcm_annotation]]
457	{mcm_annotation}::
458	A comment line providing type categorization or metadata about terms in a
459	{mcm_document}.
460	{mcm_annotation_s}
461	are ignored during first-order document reading.
462	When a referencing system is in context,
463	{mcm_annotation_s}
464	provide structured metadata interpreted by that system.
465	The content contains space-separated
466	{mcm_linked_term_s}
467	from the referencing system, which is typically out-of-context during first-order reading.
468	The grammar, interpretation, and placement rules for
469	{mcm_annotation_s}
470	are defined by the referencing system, not MCM.
471	+
472	{mcm_annotation_s}
473	use prefix-discriminated form: `//ax...` — no space after `//`, where the initial characters match a registered prefix identifying the referencing system.
474	The full content after `//` is interpreted by the referencing system as space-separated prefix terms.
475	+
476	The mechanical discriminator is the character immediately following `//`.
477	A space character indicates a regular comment.
478	A letter with no intervening space indicates an
479	{mcm_annotation}.
480	+
481	Registered prefixes:
482	+
483	* `ax` — Axial Lexicon
484	
485	[[mcm_form_deflist]]
486	{mcm_form_deflist}::
487	The standard pattern using AsciiDoc definition list syntax.
488	The anchor target appears on its own line, followed by an optional
489	{mcm_annotation}
490	then the term reference with `::` and definition text.
491	Use for standalone terms with self-contained meaning.
492	
493	[source,asciidoc]
494	----
495	[[term_anchor]]
496	//axl_voices axk_motif
497	{term_reference}::
498	Definition text following sentence-per-line rules.
499	----
500	
501	[[mcm_form_bullet]]
502	{mcm_form_bullet}::
503	A pattern for terms that belong to a parent structure or sequence.
504	Each term appears as a bullet item under its parent section.
505	The anchor target appears after the bullet marker, followed by an optional
506	{mcm_annotation}
507	then the term reference with definition prose.
508	Use for fields of a structure or steps in a procedure.
509	
510	[source,asciidoc]
511	----
512	* [[field_anchor]]
513	//axl_voices axr_member
514	{field_reference} defines the specific meaning within the parent structure.
515	----
516	
517	[[mcm_form_section]]
518	{mcm_form_section}::
519	A pattern where the section heading itself is the term.
520	The anchor target appears before the section marker, followed by an optional
521	{mcm_annotation}
522	then the section heading containing the term reference.
523	The section content constitutes the definition.
524	Use for terms whose meaning requires extended explanation or contains nested child terms.
525	
526	[source,asciidoc]
527	----
528	[[sequence_anchor]]
529	//axl_voices axo_sequence
530	=== {sequence_reference}
531	
532	The sequence executes these steps:
533	
534	* [[step_one_anchor]]
535	//axl_voices axo_step
536	First step description.
537	----
538	
539	=== Definition Organization Patterns
540	
541	The
542	{mcm_document}
543	supports three primary patterns for organizing
544	{mcm_definition_section_s}:
545	
546	==== Traditional Grouped Definitions
547	
548	[source,asciidoc]
549	----
550	== Core Terms
551	
552	[[excm_term1]]
553	{excm_term_one}:: 
554	Primary definition establishing core meaning.
555	Subsequent sentences expand understanding, though this is at most one paragraph.
556	
557	[[excm_term2]]
558	{excm_term_two}:: 
559	Related term definition showing relationships.
560	----
561	
562	==== Distributed Definitions
563	
564	Definitions may accompany relevant content sections:
565	
566	[source,asciidoc]
567	----
568	== System Components
569	
570	Here we explore key architectural elements.
571	
572	[[excm_component]]
573	{excm_sys_component}:: 
574	Definition tied directly to its context.
575	
576	The discussion continues...
577	----
578	
579	==== Domain-Specific Glossaries
580	
581	[source,asciidoc]
582	----
583	== Technical Terms
584	
585	[[excm_tech1]]
586	{excm_tech_term_one}:: 
587	Technical definition with domain focus.
588	
589	[[excm_tech2]]
590	{excm_tech_term_two}:: 
591	Related technical concept definition.
592	----
593	
594	=== {mcm_category}, {mcm_variant}, and {mcm_domain} Usage
595	
596	The
597	{mcm_category},
598	{mcm_variant},
599	and
600	{mcm_domain}
601	work together to maintain precise term relationships and variations.
602	
603	==== {mcm_category} Rules
604	
605	* Each
606	{mcm_category}
607	declares its scope in the
608	{mcm_mapping_section}
609	header
610	* {mcm_category_ies}
611	distinguish terms by conceptual role
612	* New
613	{mcm_category_ies}
614	require explicit declaration
615	
616	Example
617	{mcm_category}
618	declarations:
619	
620	[source,asciidoc]
621	----
622	// exn_: Noun concepts - entities and objects
623	// exv_: Verb concepts - actions and operations
624	----
625	
626	==== Variant Patterns
627	
628	Common
629	{mcm_variant_s}
630	follow consistent patterns:
631	
632	[source,asciidoc]
633	----
634	:exn_word:                   <<excm_word,Word>>
635	:exn_word_s:                 <<excm_word,Words>>
636	:exn_word_p:                 <<excm_word,Word's>>
637	:exv_act:                    <<excm_act,Act>>
638	:exv_act_s:                  <<excm_act,Acts>>
639	:exv_act_ed:                 <<excm_act,Acted>>
640	:exv_act_ing:                <<excm_act,Acting>>
641	----
642	
643	The
644	{mcm_mapping_scrub}
645	enforces consistent application of these patterns across the document.
646	
647	==== {mcm_domain} Pattern
648	
649	Each term definition establishes a unique anchor using snake case:
650	
651	[source,asciidoc]
652	----
653	// Domain declaration: excm_<canonical_term>
654	----
655	
656	== Task Lens Support
657	
658	{mcm_concept_model_s}
659	support focused work contexts through
660	{mcm_task_lens_es}
661	that emphasize relevant concepts while suppressing distractions.
662	See the
663	{mcm_task_lens_guide}
664	for compilation specifications and usage patterns.
665	
666	=== Task Lens Structure
667	
668	Each
669	{mcm_task_lens}
670	follows this pattern:
671	
672	[source,asciidoc]
673	----
674	=== [Task Lens Name]
675	
676	**Purpose**: [One sentence describing the work this lens enables]
677	
678	**Gestalt**: [Metaphorical description guiding term interpretation]
679	
680	**Core Activation**:
681	
682	* {linked_term} - [Aspect emphasis if needed]
683	* {linked_term} - [Aspect emphasis if needed]
684	
685	**Expanded Activation**:
686	
687	* {linked_term} - [Reduced detail focus]
688	* {linked_term} - [Reduced detail focus]
689	
690	**Suppressed Activation**:
691	
692	* List of {linked_term_s} to remain dormant
693	* Additional suppression patterns
694	
695	**Critical Relationships**:
696	
697	* {term_a} ↔ {term_b}: [What aspect matters]
698	* {term_c} → {term_d}: [Specific mechanism focus]
699	----
700	
701	Multiple
702	{mcm_task_lens_es}
703	may share terms but apply different
704	{mcm_focal_depth_s}
705	and aspect emphasis based on their purpose.
706	
707	=== Blank Line Exception for Task Lenses
708	
709	Despite the general document rule against
710	{mcm_blank_line_s}
711	around list items,
712	{mcm_task_lens}
713	sections require specific blank lines for valid asciidoc parsing:
714	
715	* One blank line after each `**Section**:` header before its list
716	* One blank line between major sections
717	* No blank lines between list items within a section
718	
719	This exception exists because asciidoc requires blank lines before bulleted lists to parse correctly, and the structured nature of
720	{mcm_task_lens_es}
721	makes the blank line placement predictable and consistent.
722	
723	== Actor Definitions
724	
725	[[mcm_editor]]
726	{mcm_editor}::
727	The human originator of
728	{mcm_concept_model_s}
729	and constraints.
730	The
731	{mcm_editor}
732	guides document evolution, establishes patterns, and directs transformations while maintaining conceptual integrity.
733	
734	[[mcm_digital_mind]]
735	{mcm_digital_mind}::
736	An agent, typically an LLM, transforming
737	{mcm_document_s}
738	under
739	{mcm_editor}
740	direction and document constraints.
741	The
742	{mcm_digital_mind}
743	performs well-defined
744	{mcm_curation_action_s}
745	while preserving document patterns and relationships.
746	
747	== {mcm_curation_action_s}
748	
749	[[mcm_curation_action]]
750	{mcm_curation_action}::
751	A process executed by a
752	{mcm_digital_mind}
753	to adjust a
754	{mcm_document}
755	as directed by the
756	{mcm_editor}.
757	Each
758	{mcm_curation_action}
759	has a specific focus and constraints, modifying only those aspects of the
760	{mcm_document}
761	that fall within its defined scope.
762	
763	[[mcm_normalization]]
764	{mcm_normalization}::
765	A
766	{mcm_curation_action}
767	that brings a
768	{mcm_document}
769	to canonical form through two phases.
770	
771	*Phase 1: Text Normalization*
772	
773	Applies consistent formatting throughout the
774	{mcm_document}
775	body:
776	
777	* One sentence per line
778	* Each
779	{mcm_linked_term}
780	on its own line
781	* Commas and punctuation attached to preceding element
782	* {mcm_blank_line_s}
783	only between paragraphs and definition entries
784	* Preserve source code block contents without modification
785	
786	*Phase 2: Mapping Section Normalization*
787	
788	Maintains strict structural integrity of the
789	{mcm_mapping_section}:
790	
791	* Preserve
792	{mcm_category}
793	comment headers as
794	{mcm_category_group}
795	delimiters
796	* Within each
797	{mcm_category_group},
798	align `<<` to the smallest multiple of 10 columns that accommodates the longest
799	{mcm_attribute_reference}
800	* Maintain alphabetical ordering by display text within each
801	{mcm_category_group}
802	* One
803	{mcm_attribute_reference}
804	per line
805	
806	[[mcm_category_group]]
807	{mcm_category_group}::
808	A contiguous block of
809	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
810	in the
811	{mcm_mapping_section}
812	preceded by a category comment header.
813	Each
814	{mcm_category_group}
815	maintains independent column alignment,
816	allowing terms to be added without affecting other groups.
817	
818	[[mcm_demotion_analysis]]
819	{mcm_demotion_analysis}::
820	A
821	{mcm_curation_action}
822	that identifies
823	{mcm_linked_term_s}
824	whose formal status may no longer justify their maintenance overhead.
825	It suggests candidates for demotion to
826	{mcm_fundamental_text}
827	based on criteria such as:
828	* Rare usage outside their own definition
829	* Lack of meaningful variation requiring
830	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
831	* Minimal role in expressing relationships between other terms
832	* Terms whose precise meaning has become self-evident in context
833	
834	[[mcm_link_validation]]
835	{mcm_link_validation}::
836	A
837	{mcm_curation_action}
838	that verifies the mechanical integrity of the
839	{mcm_document_p}
840	linking system without modification:
841	
842	* Reports
843	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
844	that lack corresponding anchors
845	* Reports anchors that lack corresponding
846	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
847	* Validates
848	{mcm_domain}
849	format compliance
850	* Identifies malformed link syntax
851	
852	This action produces a diagnostic report for
853	{mcm_editor}
854	review.
855	Repairs are performed manually or through targeted
856	{mcm_curation_action_s}.
857	
858	[[mcm_promotion_analysis]]
859	{mcm_promotion_analysis}:: 
860	A
861	{mcm_curation_action}
862	that identifies frequently occurring or conceptually significant elements in
863	{mcm_fundamental_text}
864	that warrant promotion to
865	{mcm_linked_term}
866	status.
867	It suggests candidates based on:
868	* Frequency in definitions of other
869	{mcm_linked_term_s}
870	* Use in expressing core conceptual relationships
871	* Occurrence in multiple distinct contexts
872	* Words whose precise meaning needs maintenance across document evolution
873	
874	[[mcm_term_matching]]
875	{mcm_term_matching}::
876	A
877	{mcm_curation_action}
878	that identifies instances where concepts corresponding to existing
879	{mcm_linked_term_s}
880	appear in text without proper linking:
881	* Identifies important terms that warrant linking
882	* Proposes what should be linked
883	
884	[[mcm_validation_summary]]
885	{mcm_validation_summary}::
886	An advisory report produced by
887	{mcm_link_validation}
888	that documents the state of the
889	{mcm_document}
890	without making modifications.
891	The
892	{mcm_validation_summary}
893	enables the
894	{mcm_editor}
895	to identify issues requiring attention.
896	
897	== Document Elements
898	
899	[[mcm_fundamental_text]]
900	{mcm_fundamental_text}:: 
901	The basic sentences and phrases that comprise document text.
902	{mcm_fundamental_text}
903	follows specific formatting patterns in source while maintaining natural flow in rendered output.
904	
905	[[mcm_notional_snippet]]
906	{mcm_notional_snippet}:: 
907	An exemplar that guides
908	{mcm_curation_action_s}.
909	While it may contain asciidoc noncompliance, the
910	{mcm_digital_mind}
911	uses it to understand patterns and generate compliant asciidoc output.
912	
913	[[mcm_line_break]]
914	{mcm_line_break}::
915	A deliberate separation in a
916	{mcm_document}
917	that creates clear boundaries while preserving fluid rendering.
918	The
919	{mcm_line_break}
920	appears between independent sentences, before and after
921	{mcm_linked_term_s},
922	and between fragments surrounding
923	{mcm_linked_term_s}.
924	This pattern enables clean ancestry tracking while maintaining natural document flow.
925	
926	[[mcm_blank_line]]
927	{mcm_blank_line}::
928	A purposeful demarcation in the
929	{mcm_document}
930	that separates major structural elements such as definition entries, sections, and lists.
931	The
932	{mcm_blank_line}
933	appears only between these elements, never within them.
934	
935	== Term Management Elements
936	
937	[[mcm_attribute_reference]]
938	{mcm_attribute_reference}::
939	A variable-style reference that connects a
940	{mcm_category}
941	prefixed attribute name to a specific
942	{mcm_domain}
943	and replacement text potentially with multiple
944	{mcm_variant_s}.
945	The
946	{mcm_attribute_reference}
947	forms the mechanical foundation for
948	{mcm_linked_term}
949	implementation in the
950	{mcm_document}.
951	Each
952	{mcm_attribute_reference}
953	follows strict formatting rules defined in the
954	{mcm_mapping_section}.
955	
956	[[mcm_quoin]]
957	{mcm_quoin}::
958	A formal identifier with full cataloguing in the
959	{mcm_concept_model}:
960	{mcm_attribute_reference}
961	in the
962	{mcm_mapping_section},
963	anchor at the definition site, and definition text.
964	The cornerstone that locks the structure — addressable from anywhere, tracked across the constellation.
965	A
966	{mcm_quoin}
967	is the Diptych-era term for what
968	{mcm_linked_term}
969	describes in MCM's AsciiDoc expression.
970	
971	[[mcm_inlay]]
972	{mcm_inlay}::
973	A formal identifier following the prefix naming discipline that exists only within a subdocument's scope.
974	No
975	{mcm_attribute_reference},
976	no anchor, no
977	{mcm_mapping_section}
978	entry.
979	An
980	{mcm_inlay}
981	is set permanently into a surface — recognized by its prefix pattern — but not catalogued in the parent document's lexicon.
982	Used by hierarchy markers that need to formally identify elements without elevating them to
983	{mcm_quoin}
984	status.
985	
986	[[mcm_sprue]]
987	{mcm_sprue}::
988	A literal wire-level token in backtick delimiters representing a serialized name.
989	No
990	{mcm_attribute_reference},
991	no anchor, no
992	{mcm_mapping_section}
993	entry, no prefix naming discipline required.
994	A
995	{mcm_sprue}
996	is the exact character sequence as it appears in a wire format — a JSON property key, an API field name, a protocol identifier.
997	May originate from external schemas or foreign conventions.
998	A
999	{mcm_sprue}
1000	that follows the prefix naming discipline is also an
1001	{mcm_inlay};
1002	one that does not is recognized solely by its backtick delimiters.
1003	Used by arity rules when annotations read literal wire names from following text.
1004	
1005	[[mcm_category]]
1006	{mcm_category}::
1007	A prefix mechanism that classifies
1008	{mcm_linked_term_s}
1009	by their conceptual role.
1010	Each
1011	{mcm_category}
1012	must be declared in the
1013	{mcm_mapping_section}
1014	header and follows consistent patterns for classifying terms by type (e.g., 'doc_' for document elements, 'term_' for terminology elements).
1015	
1016	[[mcm_variant]]
1017	{mcm_variant}::
1018	A systematic modification of a base term that maintains its core meaning while expressing a different form.
1019	Common
1020	{mcm_variant_s}
1021	include plurals (_s), possessives (_p), and verb conjugations (_ed, _ing), each following patterns defined in the
1022	{mcm_mapping_section}.
1023	
1024	[[mcm_domain]]
1025	{mcm_domain}::
1026	A unique identifier that anchors a term's definition within the document structure.
1027	Each
1028	{mcm_domain}
1029	uses snake case formatting and maintains consistent patterns (e.g., 'mcm_<canonical_term>') to enable reliable linking and reference.
1030	
1031	[[mcm_task_lens]]
1032	{mcm_task_lens}::
1033	A focused activation pattern within a
1034	{mcm_concept_model}
1035	that emphasizes specific terms and relationships for particular work contexts.
1036	Each
1037	{mcm_task_lens}
1038	defines which
1039	{mcm_linked_term_s}
1040	activate at what
1041	{mcm_focal_depth}
1042	and guides interpretation through gestalt descriptions and critical relationship specifications.
1043	
1044	
1045	[[mcm_task_lens_guide]]
1046	{mcm_task_lens_guide}::
1047	A separate guide document that specifies how to compile task-focused representations from a full
1048	{mcm_concept_model}.
1049	The
1050	{mcm_task_lens_guide}
1051	defines lens structure patterns, compilation mechanics, and
1052	{mcm_focal_depth}
1053	activation rules for creating work-optimized views of concept constellations.
1054	
1055	[[mcm_lens_specification]]
1056	{mcm_lens_specification}::
1057	The structured definition within a
1058	{mcm_task_lens}
1059	that categorizes
1060	{mcm_linked_term_s}
1061	by their
1062	{mcm_focal_depth}
1063	and provides aspect focusing guidance.
1064	The
1065	{mcm_lens_specification}
1066	enables mechanical compilation of task-optimized representations from the full
1067	{mcm_concept_model}.
1068	
1069	[[mcm_focal_depth]]
1070	{mcm_focal_depth}::
1071	The activation level of a
1072	{mcm_linked_term}
1073	within a
1074	{mcm_task_lens},
1075	determining how much detail and relationship information to preserve.
1076	The three
1077	{mcm_focal_depth_s}
1078	are Core (full detail with relationships), Expanded (supporting context), and Suppressed (explicitly dormant).
1079

file.content:
= Concept Model Definition
:doctype: book

// tag::mapping-section[]
//
// Category declarations:
// mcm_: Meta Concept Model
//
// Variant patterns:
//   _s: plural form
//   _p: possessive form
//   _ed: past tense (verbs)
//   _ing: progressive form (verbs)
//
// Domain declaration: mcm_<canonical_term>

:mcm_digital_mind:           <<mcm_digital_mind,Digital Mind>>
:mcm_digital_mind_s:         <<mcm_digital_mind,Digital Minds>>
:mcm_editor:                 <<mcm_editor,Editor>>
:mcm_editor_p:               <<mcm_editor,Editor's>>

:mcm_concept_model:          <<mcm_concept_model,Concept Model>>
:mcm_concept_model_s:        <<mcm_concept_model,Concept Models>>
:mcm_concept_model_p:        <<mcm_concept_model,Concept Model's>>

:mcm_category_group:         <<mcm_category_group,Category Group>>
:mcm_category_group_s:       <<mcm_category_group,Category Groups>>
:mcm_curation_action:        <<mcm_curation_action,Curation Action>>
:mcm_curation_action_s:      <<mcm_curation_action,Curation Actions>>
:mcm_demotion_analysis:      <<mcm_demotion_analysis,Demotion Analysis>>
:mcm_link_validation:        <<mcm_link_validation,Link Validation>>
:mcm_normalization:          <<mcm_normalization,Normalization>>
:mcm_promotion_analysis:     <<mcm_promotion_analysis,Promotion Analysis>>
:mcm_term_matching:          <<mcm_term_matching,Term Matching>>
:mcm_validation_summary:     <<mcm_validation_summary,Validation Summary>>

:mcm_definition_section:     <<mcm_definition_section,Definition Section>>
:mcm_definition_section_s:   <<mcm_definition_section,Definition Sections>>
:mcm_document:               <<mcm_document,Document>>
:mcm_document_s:             <<mcm_document,Documents>>
:mcm_document_p:             <<mcm_document,Document's>>
:mcm_fundamental_text:       <<mcm_fundamental_text,Fundamental Text Organization>>
:mcm_mapping_section:        <<mcm_mapping_section,Mapping Section>>
:mcm_notional_snippet:       <<mcm_notional_snippet,Notional Snippet>>
:mcm_notional_snippet_s:     <<mcm_notional_snippet,Notional Snippets>>

:mcm_linked_term:            <<mcm_linked_term,Linked Term>>
:mcm_linked_term_s:          <<mcm_linked_term,Linked Terms>>
:mcm_quoin:                  <<mcm_quoin,Quoin>>
:mcm_quoin_s:                <<mcm_quoin,Quoins>>
:mcm_inlay:                  <<mcm_inlay,Inlay>>
:mcm_inlay_s:                <<mcm_inlay,Inlays>>
:mcm_sprue:                  <<mcm_sprue,Sprue>>
:mcm_sprue_s:                <<mcm_sprue,Sprues>>
:mcm_attribute_reference:    <<mcm_attribute_reference,Attribute Reference>>
:mcm_attribute_reference_s:  <<mcm_attribute_reference,Attribute References>>
:mcm_line_break:             <<mcm_line_break,Line Break>>
:mcm_line_break_s:           <<mcm_line_break,Line Breaks>>
:mcm_blank_line:             <<mcm_blank_line,Blank Line>>
:mcm_blank_line_s:           <<mcm_blank_line,Blank Lines>>

:mcm_category:               <<mcm_category,Link Category>>
:mcm_category_ies:           <<mcm_category,Link Categories>>
:mcm_variant:                <<mcm_variant,Link Variant>>  
:mcm_variant_s:              <<mcm_variant,Link Variants>>
:mcm_domain:                 <<mcm_domain,Link Domain>>
:mcm_domain_s:               <<mcm_domain,Link Domains>>

:mcm_task_lens:              <<mcm_task_lens,Task Lens>>
:mcm_task_lens_es:           <<mcm_task_lens,Task Lenses>>
:mcm_task_lens_guide:        <<mcm_task_lens_guide,Task Lens Guide>>
:mcm_lens_specification:     <<mcm_lens_specification,Lens Specification>>
:mcm_lens_specification_s:   <<mcm_lens_specification,Lens Specifications>>
:mcm_focal_depth:            <<mcm_focal_depth,Focal Depth>>
:mcm_focal_depth_s:          <<mcm_focal_depth,Focal Depths>>

:mcm_annotation:             <<mcm_annotation,Annotation>>
:mcm_annotation_s:           <<mcm_annotation,Annotations>>
:mcm_form_deflist:           <<mcm_form_deflist,Definition List Form>>
:mcm_form_bullet:            <<mcm_form_bullet,Bullet Form>>
:mcm_form_section:           <<mcm_form_section,Section Form>>

// end::mapping-section[]

== Core Structure

A
{mcm_concept_model}
creates a cohesive gestalt - a unified constellation of ideas whose relationships and interactions transcend their individual definitions.
Through carefully curated terminology and precise linking patterns, each
{mcm_concept_model}
illuminates the deep conceptual relationships that give meaning to a domain.

The power of a
{mcm_concept_model}
lies in its ability to:

* Reveal the natural connections between ideas
* Preserve the integrity of concept relationships through evolution
* Enable clear visualization of how changes to one concept ripple through the constellation
* Guide understanding through carefully mapped idea spaces

Each
{mcm_concept_model}
manifests through a
{mcm_document}
that follows strict organizational patterns.
These patterns ensure that as the constellation evolves, their relationships remain clear and traceable.

The
{mcm_document}
contains two primary constrained element types:

* A
{mcm_mapping_section}
that defines all
{mcm_linked_term_s}
* One or more
{mcm_definition_section_s}
that provide precise meaning for each term

Through these elements, the
{mcm_document}
maintains the delicate balance between rigorous structure and organic concept evolution, allowing the idea constellation to grow while preserving its essential relationships.

== Text Organization

Text within a
{mcm_document}
follows specific structural rules to enable clear tracking of changes and relationships.

=== Line Break Fundamentals

The most fundamental organizing principle is the
{mcm_line_break}
.
Each
{mcm_line_break}
creates a clear boundary in the
{mcm_document}
while preserving fluid rendering in the output document.

==== Basic Line Break Rules

{mcm_line_break_s}
occur in these precise patterns:

* Between independent sentences
* Before and after each
{mcm_linked_term}
(including each term within a sequence)
* Between fragments that surround
{mcm_linked_term_s}

Basic example showing sentence and term breaks:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The system initializes its components.
The
{excm_processor}
begins operation.
Each component reports its status.
----

==== Term Integration Patterns

When incorporating
{mcm_linked_term_s}
the text follows these specific patterns:

Single term integration - breaks before and after the term:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The system processes
{excm_request}
according to defined rules.
----

Multiple
{mcm_linked_term_s}
in sequence - each term gets its own line, with commas attached:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The interface supports
{excm_drag},
{excm_drop},
and
{excm_scroll}
operations.
----

Complex
{mcm_linked_term}
relationships - each standalone term gets breaks:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The
{excm_validator}
applies each
{excm_rule}
to ensure
{excm_compliance}
with system requirements.
----

These patterns apply consistently across all document contexts, including:

* Regular paragraphs
* List items
* Definition entries
* Section introductions

=== Paragraph Organization

Paragraphs in the
{mcm_document}
follow these structural principles:

* {mcm_blank_line_s}
appear only between true paragraphs
* No
{mcm_blank_line_s}
between sentences within a paragraph
* No
{mcm_blank_line_s}
around source code blocks or list items
* Each paragraph focuses on a single concept or relationship

Example paragraph structure:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The system initializes its core components.
Each component performs self-diagnostics.
The
{excm_validator}
confirms system health.

The initialization process then moves to external systems.
The
{excm_connector}
establishes required links.
Each connection undergoes verification.
----

=== Special Cases

==== List Items

The
{mcm_document}
presents list items
with proper term integration
and conceptual breaks:

[source,asciidoc]
----
* The first item spans
{excm_component}
boundaries
* A second item demonstrates
{excm_process}
flow
* The final item shows
{excm_result}
formatting
----

==== Definition Entries

Definition entries follow standard sentence breaks while maintaining definition list structure:

The single space following `::` is necessary.

[source,asciidoc]
----
[[excm_term]]
{excm_example}:: 
The primary definition establishes core meaning.
Additional sentences expand understanding.
The
{excm_linked_term}
shows relationship handling.
----

==== Source Code Boundaries

Source code blocks establish strict boundaries for
{mcm_curation_action_s}.
These boundaries protect code content while maintaining proper term integration in surrounding text:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The
{excm_processor}
implements this interface:

[source,java]
-----
public interface Processor {
    void process();
    Status check();
}
-----

The implementation ensures
{excm_compliance}
with system requirements.
----

{mcm_curation_action_s}
respect these boundaries by:

* Maintaining term integration and line breaks in surrounding text
* Treating code block contents as opaque
* Preserving code formatting without modification
* Excluding code content from term matching and promotion analysis

This boundary pattern ensures code examples remain stable through document evolution while maintaining proper conceptual integration in their context.

=== Punctuation Rules

Punctuation follows standard English conventions regardless of line breaks:

* Sentence-ending punctuation stays with the sentence
* Commas remain with their preceding phrase
* Quotation marks enclose their content without breaks
* Parentheses maintain content unity

Example punctuation handling:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The system (version 2.0) activates its components.
The
{excm_processor}
begins its "primary sequence."
After initialization,
the system reports "ready."
----

=== Document Mechanics

=== {mcm_mapping_section} Constraints

The
{mcm_mapping_section}
enforces alignment rules to enhance readability and change tracking:

* The `<<` of each
{mcm_domain}
aligns to columns that are multiples of 10
* When entries require wider alignment, all lines adjust to the next larger multiple
* {mcm_attribute_reference_s}
maintain consistent indentation

[source,asciidoc]
----
// tag::mapping-example[]
:excm_doc_short:              <<excm_short,Short Term>>
:excm_doc_longer_term:        <<excm_longer,Longer Term>>
:excm_doc_much_longer_term:   <<excm_much_longer,Much Longer Term>>
// end::mapping-example[]
----

The
{mcm_mapping_scrub}
maintains these alignments during curation unless expansion is required.

=== Document Source Management

The
{mcm_document}
maintains compatibility with line-based tools
while preserving semantic structure.

This means:

* Favoring AsciiDoc's native list markers
over hardcoded sequence numbers

* Preserving processor directives
that enable clean rendering

* Ensuring changes remain visible
in both source differencing tools
and rendered comparisons

The goal is maintaining a document that:

* Shows ancestry clearly in source
* Renders cleanly through AsciiDoc
* Displays changes meaningfully in diffs
* Supports post-rendering analysis

=== Term Hierarchy Management

When
{mcm_linked_term_s}
appear to form hierarchical relationships, the
{mcm_document}
flattens this hierarchy through specific patterns:

* Each
{mcm_linked_term_s}
receives first-class presentation in the
{mcm_mapping_section}
* Related
{mcm_linked_term_s}
maintain separate definitions
* Relationships express through careful definition crafting

Example of hierarchical
{mcm_linked_term_s}
handling:

[source,asciidoc]
----
:excm_doc_validation:             <<excm_validation,Validation>>
:excm_doc_validation_rule:        <<excm_validation_rule,Validation Rule>>
:excm_doc_validation_set:         <<excm_validation_set,Validation Set>>
----

The definitions then express relationships:

[source,asciidoc]
----
[[excm_validation]]
{excm_doc_validation}:: 
The process of applying rules to ensure content correctness.

[[excm_validation_rule]]
{excm_doc_validation_rule}:: 
A specific constraint within the {excm_doc_validation} framework.

[[excm_validation_set]]
{excm_doc_validation_set}:: 
A collection of {excm_doc_validation_rule_s} that work together.
----

== Definition Patterns

=== Definition Form Patterns

Each
{mcm_linked_term}
in a
{mcm_document}
requires an anchor target and a definition.
The form determines how these elements are structured.

[[mcm_annotation]]
{mcm_annotation}::
A comment line providing type categorization or metadata about terms in a
{mcm_document}.
{mcm_annotation_s}
are ignored during first-order document reading.
When a referencing system is in context,
{mcm_annotation_s}
provide structured metadata interpreted by that system.
The content contains space-separated
{mcm_linked_term_s}
from the referencing system, which is typically out-of-context during first-order reading.
The grammar, interpretation, and placement rules for
{mcm_annotation_s}
are defined by the referencing system, not MCM.
+
{mcm_annotation_s}
use prefix-discriminated form: `//ax...` — no space after `//`, where the initial characters match a registered prefix identifying the referencing system.
The full content after `//` is interpreted by the referencing system as space-separated prefix terms.
+
The mechanical discriminator is the character immediately following `//`.
A space character indicates a regular comment.
A letter with no intervening space indicates an
{mcm_annotation}.
+
Registered prefixes:
+
* `ax` — Axial Lexicon

[[mcm_form_deflist]]
{mcm_form_deflist}::
The standard pattern using AsciiDoc definition list syntax.
The anchor target appears on its own line, followed by an optional
{mcm_annotation}
then the term reference with `::` and definition text.
Use for standalone terms with self-contained meaning.

[source,asciidoc]
----
[[term_anchor]]
//axl_voices axk_motif
{term_reference}::
Definition text following sentence-per-line rules.
----

[[mcm_form_bullet]]
{mcm_form_bullet}::
A pattern for terms that belong to a parent structure or sequence.
Each term appears as a bullet item under its parent section.
The anchor target appears after the bullet marker, followed by an optional
{mcm_annotation}
then the term reference with definition prose.
Use for fields of a structure or steps in a procedure.

[source,asciidoc]
----
* [[field_anchor]]
//axl_voices axr_member
{field_reference} defines the specific meaning within the parent structure.
----

[[mcm_form_section]]
{mcm_form_section}::
A pattern where the section heading itself is the term.
The anchor target appears before the section marker, followed by an optional
{mcm_annotation}
then the section heading containing the term reference.
The section content constitutes the definition.
Use for terms whose meaning requires extended explanation or contains nested child terms.

[source,asciidoc]
----
[[sequence_anchor]]
//axl_voices axo_sequence
=== {sequence_reference}

The sequence executes these steps:

* [[step_one_anchor]]
//axl_voices axo_step
First step description.
----

=== Definition Organization Patterns

The
{mcm_document}
supports three primary patterns for organizing
{mcm_definition_section_s}:

==== Traditional Grouped Definitions

[source,asciidoc]
----
== Core Terms

[[excm_term1]]
{excm_term_one}:: 
Primary definition establishing core meaning.
Subsequent sentences expand understanding, though this is at most one paragraph.

[[excm_term2]]
{excm_term_two}:: 
Related term definition showing relationships.
----

==== Distributed Definitions

Definitions may accompany relevant content sections:

[source,asciidoc]
----
== System Components

Here we explore key architectural elements.

[[excm_component]]
{excm_sys_component}:: 
Definition tied directly to its context.

The discussion continues...
----

==== Domain-Specific Glossaries

[source,asciidoc]
----
== Technical Terms

[[excm_tech1]]
{excm_tech_term_one}:: 
Technical definition with domain focus.

[[excm_tech2]]
{excm_tech_term_two}:: 
Related technical concept definition.
----

=== {mcm_category}, {mcm_variant}, and {mcm_domain} Usage

The
{mcm_category},
{mcm_variant},
and
{mcm_domain}
work together to maintain precise term relationships and variations.

==== {mcm_category} Rules

* Each
{mcm_category}
declares its scope in the
{mcm_mapping_section}
header
* {mcm_category_ies}
distinguish terms by conceptual role
* New
{mcm_category_ies}
require explicit declaration

Example
{mcm_category}
declarations:

[source,asciidoc]
----
// exn_: Noun concepts - entities and objects
// exv_: Verb concepts - actions and operations
----

==== Variant Patterns

Common
{mcm_variant_s}
follow consistent patterns:

[source,asciidoc]
----
:exn_word:                   <<excm_word,Word>>
:exn_word_s:                 <<excm_word,Words>>
:exn_word_p:                 <<excm_word,Word's>>
:exv_act:                    <<excm_act,Act>>
:exv_act_s:                  <<excm_act,Acts>>
:exv_act_ed:                 <<excm_act,Acted>>
:exv_act_ing:                <<excm_act,Acting>>
----

The
{mcm_mapping_scrub}
enforces consistent application of these patterns across the document.

==== {mcm_domain} Pattern

Each term definition establishes a unique anchor using snake case:

[source,asciidoc]
----
// Domain declaration: excm_<canonical_term>
----

== Task Lens Support

{mcm_concept_model_s}
support focused work contexts through
{mcm_task_lens_es}
that emphasize relevant concepts while suppressing distractions.
See the
{mcm_task_lens_guide}
for compilation specifications and usage patterns.

=== Task Lens Structure

Each
{mcm_task_lens}
follows this pattern:

[source,asciidoc]
----
=== [Task Lens Name]

**Purpose**: [One sentence describing the work this lens enables]

**Gestalt**: [Metaphorical description guiding term interpretation]

**Core Activation**:

* {linked_term} - [Aspect emphasis if needed]
* {linked_term} - [Aspect emphasis if needed]

**Expanded Activation**:

* {linked_term} - [Reduced detail focus]
* {linked_term} - [Reduced detail focus]

**Suppressed Activation**:

* List of {linked_term_s} to remain dormant
* Additional suppression patterns

**Critical Relationships**:

* {term_a} ↔ {term_b}: [What aspect matters]
* {term_c} → {term_d}: [Specific mechanism focus]
----

Multiple
{mcm_task_lens_es}
may share terms but apply different
{mcm_focal_depth_s}
and aspect emphasis based on their purpose.

=== Blank Line Exception for Task Lenses

Despite the general document rule against
{mcm_blank_line_s}
around list items,
{mcm_task_lens}
sections require specific blank lines for valid asciidoc parsing:

* One blank line after each `**Section**:` header before its list
* One blank line between major sections
* No blank lines between list items within a section

This exception exists because asciidoc requires blank lines before bulleted lists to parse correctly, and the structured nature of
{mcm_task_lens_es}
makes the blank line placement predictable and consistent.

== Actor Definitions

[[mcm_editor]]
{mcm_editor}::
The human originator of
{mcm_concept_model_s}
and constraints.
The
{mcm_editor}
guides document evolution, establishes patterns, and directs transformations while maintaining conceptual integrity.

[[mcm_digital_mind]]
{mcm_digital_mind}::
An agent, typically an LLM, transforming
{mcm_document_s}
under
{mcm_editor}
direction and document constraints.
The
{mcm_digital_mind}
performs well-defined
{mcm_curation_action_s}
while preserving document patterns and relationships.

== {mcm_curation_action_s}

[[mcm_curation_action]]
{mcm_curation_action}::
A process executed by a
{mcm_digital_mind}
to adjust a
{mcm_document}
as directed by the
{mcm_editor}.
Each
{mcm_curation_action}
has a specific focus and constraints, modifying only those aspects of the
{mcm_document}
that fall within its defined scope.

[[mcm_normalization]]
{mcm_normalization}::
A
{mcm_curation_action}
that brings a
{mcm_document}
to canonical form through two phases.

*Phase 1: Text Normalization*

Applies consistent formatting throughout the
{mcm_document}
body:

* One sentence per line
* Each
{mcm_linked_term}
on its own line
* Commas and punctuation attached to preceding element
* {mcm_blank_line_s}
only between paragraphs and definition entries
* Preserve source code block contents without modification

*Phase 2: Mapping Section Normalization*

Maintains strict structural integrity of the
{mcm_mapping_section}:

* Preserve
{mcm_category}
comment headers as
{mcm_category_group}
delimiters
* Within each
{mcm_category_group},
align `<<` to the smallest multiple of 10 columns that accommodates the longest
{mcm_attribute_reference}
* Maintain alphabetical ordering by display text within each
{mcm_category_group}
* One
{mcm_attribute_reference}
per line

[[mcm_category_group]]
{mcm_category_group}::
A contiguous block of
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
in the
{mcm_mapping_section}
preceded by a category comment header.
Each
{mcm_category_group}
maintains independent column alignment,
allowing terms to be added without affecting other groups.

[[mcm_demotion_analysis]]
{mcm_demotion_analysis}::
A
{mcm_curation_action}
that identifies
{mcm_linked_term_s}
whose formal status may no longer justify their maintenance overhead.
It suggests candidates for demotion to
{mcm_fundamental_text}
based on criteria such as:
* Rare usage outside their own definition
* Lack of meaningful variation requiring
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
* Minimal role in expressing relationships between other terms
* Terms whose precise meaning has become self-evident in context

[[mcm_link_validation]]
{mcm_link_validation}::
A
{mcm_curation_action}
that verifies the mechanical integrity of the
{mcm_document_p}
linking system without modification:

* Reports
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
that lack corresponding anchors
* Reports anchors that lack corresponding
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
* Validates
{mcm_domain}
format compliance
* Identifies malformed link syntax

This action produces a diagnostic report for
{mcm_editor}
review.
Repairs are performed manually or through targeted
{mcm_curation_action_s}.

[[mcm_promotion_analysis]]
{mcm_promotion_analysis}:: 
A
{mcm_curation_action}
that identifies frequently occurring or conceptually significant elements in
{mcm_fundamental_text}
that warrant promotion to
{mcm_linked_term}
status.
It suggests candidates based on:
* Frequency in definitions of other
{mcm_linked_term_s}
* Use in expressing core conceptual relationships
* Occurrence in multiple distinct contexts
* Words whose precise meaning needs maintenance across document evolution

[[mcm_term_matching]]
{mcm_term_matching}::
A
{mcm_curation_action}
that identifies instances where concepts corresponding to existing
{mcm_linked_term_s}
appear in text without proper linking:
* Identifies important terms that warrant linking
* Proposes what should be linked

[[mcm_validation_summary]]
{mcm_validation_summary}::
An advisory report produced by
{mcm_link_validation}
that documents the state of the
{mcm_document}
without making modifications.
The
{mcm_validation_summary}
enables the
{mcm_editor}
to identify issues requiring attention.

== Document Elements

[[mcm_fundamental_text]]
{mcm_fundamental_text}:: 
The basic sentences and phrases that comprise document text.
{mcm_fundamental_text}
follows specific formatting patterns in source while maintaining natural flow in rendered output.

[[mcm_notional_snippet]]
{mcm_notional_snippet}:: 
An exemplar that guides
{mcm_curation_action_s}.
While it may contain asciidoc noncompliance, the
{mcm_digital_mind}
uses it to understand patterns and generate compliant asciidoc output.

[[mcm_line_break]]
{mcm_line_break}::
A deliberate separation in a
{mcm_document}
that creates clear boundaries while preserving fluid rendering.
The
{mcm_line_break}
appears between independent sentences, before and after
{mcm_linked_term_s},
and between fragments surrounding
{mcm_linked_term_s}.
This pattern enables clean ancestry tracking while maintaining natural document flow.

[[mcm_blank_line]]
{mcm_blank_line}::
A purposeful demarcation in the
{mcm_document}
that separates major structural elements such as definition entries, sections, and lists.
The
{mcm_blank_line}
appears only between these elements, never within them.

== Term Management Elements

[[mcm_attribute_reference]]
{mcm_attribute_reference}::
A variable-style reference that connects a
{mcm_category}
prefixed attribute name to a specific
{mcm_domain}
and replacement text potentially with multiple
{mcm_variant_s}.
The
{mcm_attribute_reference}
forms the mechanical foundation for
{mcm_linked_term}
implementation in the
{mcm_document}.
Each
{mcm_attribute_reference}
follows strict formatting rules defined in the
{mcm_mapping_section}.

[[mcm_quoin]]
{mcm_quoin}::
A formal identifier with full cataloguing in the
{mcm_concept_model}:
{mcm_attribute_reference}
in the
{mcm_mapping_section},
anchor at the definition site, and definition text.
The cornerstone that locks the structure — addressable from anywhere, tracked across the constellation.
A
{mcm_quoin}
is the Diptych-era term for what
{mcm_linked_term}
describes in MCM's AsciiDoc expression.

[[mcm_inlay]]
{mcm_inlay}::
A formal identifier following the prefix naming discipline that exists only within a subdocument's scope.
No
{mcm_attribute_reference},
no anchor, no
{mcm_mapping_section}
entry.
An
{mcm_inlay}
is set permanently into a surface — recognized by its prefix pattern — but not catalogued in the parent document's lexicon.
Used by hierarchy markers that need to formally identify elements without elevating them to
{mcm_quoin}
status.

[[mcm_sprue]]
{mcm_sprue}::
A literal wire-level token in backtick delimiters representing a serialized name.
No
{mcm_attribute_reference},
no anchor, no
{mcm_mapping_section}
entry, no prefix naming discipline required.
A
{mcm_sprue}
is the exact character sequence as it appears in a wire format — a JSON property key, an API field name, a protocol identifier.
May originate from external schemas or foreign conventions.
A
{mcm_sprue}
that follows the prefix naming discipline is also an
{mcm_inlay};
one that does not is recognized solely by its backtick delimiters.
Used by arity rules when annotations read literal wire names from following text.

[[mcm_category]]
{mcm_category}::
A prefix mechanism that classifies
{mcm_linked_term_s}
by their conceptual role.
Each
{mcm_category}
must be declared in the
{mcm_mapping_section}
header and follows consistent patterns for classifying terms by type (e.g., 'doc_' for document elements, 'term_' for terminology elements).

[[mcm_variant]]
{mcm_variant}::
A systematic modification of a base term that maintains its core meaning while expressing a different form.
Common
{mcm_variant_s}
include plurals (_s), possessives (_p), and verb conjugations (_ed, _ing), each following patterns defined in the
{mcm_mapping_section}.

[[mcm_domain]]
{mcm_domain}::
A unique identifier that anchors a term's definition within the document structure.
Each
{mcm_domain}
uses snake case formatting and maintains consistent patterns (e.g., 'mcm_<canonical_term>') to enable reliable linking and reference.

[[mcm_task_lens]]
{mcm_task_lens}::
A focused activation pattern within a
{mcm_concept_model}
that emphasizes specific terms and relationships for particular work contexts.
Each
{mcm_task_lens}
defines which
{mcm_linked_term_s}
activate at what
{mcm_focal_depth}
and guides interpretation through gestalt descriptions and critical relationship specifications.


[[mcm_task_lens_guide]]
{mcm_task_lens_guide}::
A separate guide document that specifies how to compile task-focused representations from a full
{mcm_concept_model}.
The
{mcm_task_lens_guide}
defines lens structure patterns, compilation mechanics, and
{mcm_focal_depth}
activation rules for creating work-optimized views of concept constellations.

[[mcm_lens_specification]]
{mcm_lens_specification}::
The structured definition within a
{mcm_task_lens}
that categorizes
{mcm_linked_term_s}
by their
{mcm_focal_depth}
and provides aspect focusing guidance.
The
{mcm_lens_specification}
enables mechanical compilation of task-optimized representations from the full
{mcm_concept_model}.

[[mcm_focal_depth]]
{mcm_focal_depth}::
The activation level of a
{mcm_linked_term}
within a
{mcm_task_lens},
determining how much detail and relationship information to preserve.
The three
{mcm_focal_depth_s}
are Core (full detail with relationships), Expanded (supporting context), and Suppressed (explicitly dormant).
