1145	Example usage in an operation:
1146	+
1147	[source,asciidoc]
1148	----
1149	{jjds_arguments}
1150	
1151	//axd_required
1152	* {jjda_silks}
1153	
1154	//axd_optional axd_defaulted
1155	* {jjda_file}
1156	----
1157	
1158	[[axa_cli_option]]
1159	{axa_cli_option}::
1160	A
1161	{axl_motif}
1162	representing a command-line option definition.
1163	{axl_voicing_s}
1164	require the definition text to specify:
1165	+
1166	* The long form (e.g., `--file`)
1167	* The short form, or its absence (e.g., `-f` or "none")
1168	+
1169	Example:
1170	+
1171	[source,asciidoc]
1172	----
1173	[[jjda_file]]
1174	//axl_voices axa_cli_option
1175	{jjda_file}::
1176	Path to the Gallops JSON file.
1177	Long: `--file`.
1178	Short: none.
1179	Default: `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
1180	----
1181	
1182	[[axa_exit_uniform]]
1183	{axa_exit_uniform}::
1184	A
1185	{axl_motif}
1186	representing an Exit Status section that follows standard Unix conventions.
1187	{axl_voices}
1188	{axhoc_completion}
1189	in CLI context.
1190	Exit 0 indicates success; any non-zero exit indicates error.
1191	{axl_voicing_s}
1192	may use terse notation since the pattern is conventional.
1193	The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
1194	Specifications define separate section header terms for uniform vs enumerated exit patterns.
1195	+
1196	Example section header definition:
1197	+
1198	[source,asciidoc]
1199	----
1200	[[jjds_exit_uniform]]
1201	//axl_voices axa_exit_uniform
1202	{jjds_exit_uniform}::
1203	Section header for operations with standard exit semantics.
1204	----
1205	+
1206	Example usage:
1207	+
1208	[source,asciidoc]
1209	----
1210	{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
1211	----
1212	
1213	[[axa_exit_enumerated]]
1214	{axa_exit_enumerated}::
1215	A
1216	{axl_motif}
1217	representing an Exit Status section with distinct exit codes carrying specific meanings.
1218	{axl_voices}
1219	{axhoc_completion}
1220	in CLI context.
1221	{axl_voicing_s}
1222	require the definition text to list each exit code and its meaning.
1223	The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
1224	Specifications define separate section header terms for uniform vs enumerated exit patterns.
1225	+
1226	Example section header definition:
1227	+
1228	[source,asciidoc]
1229	----
1230	[[jjds_exit_enumerated]]
1231	//axl_voices axa_exit_enumerated
1232	{jjds_exit_enumerated}::
1233	Section header for operations with distinct exit codes.
1234	----
1235	+
1236	Example usage:
1237	+
1238	[source,asciidoc]
1239	----
1240	{jjds_exit_enumerated}
1241	
1242	* 0 — found and valid
1243	* 1 — not found
1244	* 2 — found but malformed
1245	----
1246	
1247	== Axial Interface Terms
1248	
1249	[[axi_command_interface]]
1250	{axi_command_interface}::
1251	A
1252	{axl_motif}
1253	representing complete specification of a command's invocation pattern.
1254	Defines syntax and semantics for command usage.
1255	
1256	[[axi_subcommand]]
1257	{axi_subcommand}::
1258	A
1259	{axl_motif}
1260	representing nested command within parent command structure.
1261	Inherits context from parent command.
1262	
1263	[[axi_command_signature]]
1264	{axi_command_signature}::
1265	A
1266	{axl_motif}
1267	representing complete parameter specification for a command.
1268	Serves as contract for command invocation.
1269	
1270	[[axi_endpoint]]
1271	{axi_endpoint}::
1272	A
1273	{axl_motif}
1274	representing URL path accepting HTTP requests.
1275	Defines resource location and accepted operations.
1276	
1277	[[axi_api_method]]
1278	{axi_api_method}::
1279	A
1280	{axl_motif}
1281	representing HTTP verb combined with
1282	{axi_endpoint}
1283	specification.
1284	Includes request and response specifications.
1285	
1286	[[axi_url_parameter]]
1287	{axi_url_parameter}::
1288	A
1289	{axl_motif}
1290	representing query string key-value pair in URL.
1291	Appears after `?` in URL structure.
1292	
1293	[[axi_hash_parameter]]
1294	{axi_hash_parameter}::
1295	A
1296	{axl_motif}
1297	representing fragment identifier for client-side state.
1298	Persists state without server round-trips.
1299	
1300	[[axi_request_body]]
1301	{axi_request_body}::
1302	A
1303	{axl_motif}
1304	representing structured input payload for HTTP requests.
1305	Validated against
1306	{axj_structure}
1307	definitions.
1308	
1309	[[axi_response_body]]
1310	{axi_response_body}::
1311	A
1312	{axl_motif}
1313	representing structured output payload from HTTP responses.
1314	Conforms to documented
1315	{axj_structure}.
1316	
1317	[[axi_cli_interface]]
1318	{axi_cli_interface}::
1319	A
1320	{axl_motif}
1321	representing command-line invocation pattern specification.
1322	Documents
1323	{axa_argument_s}
1324	and behavior.
1325	
1326	[[axi_cli_program]]
1327	{axi_cli_program}::
1328	A
1329	{axl_motif}
1330	representing a Unix-style command-line program.
1331	Invoked from a shell with arguments.
1332	Writes results to stdout, errors to stderr.
1333	Returns exit status: 0 for success, non-zero for failure.
1334	Suitable for scripted, non-interactive use.
1335	
1336	[[axi_cli_command_group]]
1337	{axi_cli_command_group}::
1338	A
1339	{axl_motif}
1340	representing a namespace within a CLI that contains subcommands.
1341	Does not perform operations itself; serves as organizational container.
1342	May be nested within an
1343	{axi_cli_program}
1344	or another
1345	{axi_cli_command_group}.
1346	Inherits Unix CLI semantics from parent.
1347	
1348	[[axi_cli_subcommand]]
1349	{axi_cli_subcommand}::
1350	A
1351	{axl_motif}
1352	representing a leaf operation within an
1353	{axi_cli_program}
1354	or
1355	{axi_cli_command_group}.
1356	Performs an actual operation; not a container for other commands.
1357	Inherits Unix CLI semantics from parent: arguments, stdout, stderr, exit status.
1358	
1359	[[axi_rest_api]]
1360	{axi_rest_api}::
1361	A
1362	{axl_motif}
1363	representing resource-based web service interface.
1364	Maps operations to HTTP methods and resources.
1365	
1366	[[axi_rpc_interface]]
1367	{axi_rpc_interface}::
1368	A
1369	{axl_motif}
1370	representing remote procedure call pattern for service invocation.
1371	Emphasizes operation semantics over HTTP methods.
1372	
1373	[[axi_sse_endpoint]]
1374	{axi_sse_endpoint}::
1375	A
1376	{axl_motif}
1377	representing server-sent event stream for push notifications.
1378	Enables real-time updates to connected clients.
1379	
1380	[[axi_cc_slash_command]]
1381	{axi_cc_slash_command}::
1382	A
1383	{axl_motif}
1384	representing a Claude Code slash command skill artifact.
1385	Implemented as a `.claude/commands/*.md` file containing orchestration logic that the LLM executes as a skill.
1386	Slash commands coordinate multiple
1387	{axi_cli_subcommand}
1388	invocations, gather context, and apply workflow policies.
1389	
1390	[[axi_cc_claudemd_verb]]
1391	{axi_cc_claudemd_verb}::
1392	A
1393	{axl_motif}
1394	representing an action verb defined in a
1395	{axi_cc_claudemd_section}
1396	that maps user intent to tool-layer operations.
1397	The verb exists as a concept in the LLM context — either mapping directly to a single
1398	{axi_cli_subcommand}
1399	(direct verb) or orchestrating multiple operations (multistep verb implemented by a
1400	{axi_cc_slash_command}).
1401	
1402	[[axi_cc_claudemd_section]]
1403	{axi_cc_claudemd_section}::
1404	A
1405	{axl_motif}
1406	representing a managed section within a project CLAUDE.md file.
1407	Provides persistent LLM context that bridges specification-layer concepts to runtime execution.
1408	Typically contains command references, vocabulary mappings, and operational guidance.
1409	
1410	[[axi_protocol]]
1411	{axi_protocol}::
1412	A
1413	{axl_motif}
1414	representing the bilateral discipline binding two
1415	{axo_actor_s}
1416	— a caller and a callee — through a defined sequence of call and return envelopes.
1417	Distinct from
1418	{axi_rpc_interface}
1419	which describes one side's view of a service;
1420	{axi_protocol}
1421	is the relationship itself, owning transport, lifecycle, and concurrency presumptions.
1422	A protocol quoin carries transport via
1423	{axd_dimension_s}
1424	(e.g.,
1425	{axd_file_bus}),
1426	lifecycle presumptions (e.g.,
1427	{axd_disjoint_lifecycle}),
1428	and concurrency discipline as dimensions on the primary voicing.
1429	Structural detail — the roster of transactions, call/return envelopes, and member types — lives at the
1430	{axl_detail_site}
1431	under
1432	{axhpb_protocol}
1433	and nested hierarchy markers.
1434

file.content:
== Axial Control Terms

[[axc_variable]]
{axc_variable}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a temporary value holder denoted by distinctive delimiters that exists for the duration of an operational sequence.
Implementation varies by context: shell variables in bash, dictionary entries in Python, sessionStorage in browser JavaScript.

[[axc_fatal]]
{axc_fatal}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing immediate termination of the operational sequence with error status, preventing all subsequent steps from executing.
Requires operator intervention or error recovery before retry.

[[axc_warn]]
{axc_warn}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing display of a non-blocking warning message while allowing the operational sequence to continue execution.
Alerts operators to concerning conditions without halting progress.

[[axc_store]]
{axc_store}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing capture of a value from an operation result or computation into a
{axc_variable}
for later reference.
Context determines storage mechanism while preserving semantic intent.

[[axc_use]]
{axc_use}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing reference to a previously stored
{axc_variable}
value in the current operation.
Retrieves stored values from context-appropriate storage location.

[[axc_require]]
{axc_require}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing validation check that confirms an expected condition, triggering
{axc_fatal}
or alternate execution path if not met.
Enforces preconditions and invariants throughout execution.

[[axc_poll]]
{axc_poll}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing repeated execution of a status check at defined intervals until completion condition or timeout.
Implements retry logic with configurable intervals and limits.

[[axc_call]]
{axc_call}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing direct invocation of an external service, API endpoint, or command.
Captures both success and error responses for processing.

[[axc_submit]]
{axc_submit}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing initiation of an asynchronous operation that returns immediately with a handle for later completion tracking.
Distinct from
{axc_call}
which expects synchronous response.

[[axc_await]]
{axc_await}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing blocking wait on a submitted asynchronous operation until completion.
Polls operation status until done, then returns final result or error.

[[axc_show]]
{axc_show}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing presentation of operational facts or values to the operator.
Implementation varies from console output to UI display based on context.

[[axc_step]]
{axc_step}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a normal progress message indicating successful completion of a processing stage.
Provides operational visibility without implying warnings or errors.

== Axial Environment Terms

{axe_environment_s}
specify execution contexts for control operations.
{axl_voicing_s}
of
{axc_call},
{axc_warn},
{axc_fatal}
and similar control motifs describe step-level behavior within operations.

[[axe_rest_api]]
{axe_rest_api}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing invocation of an HTTP REST API endpoint.
Request/response via JSON or other structured format.
Authentication via bearer tokens, API keys, or similar.
Calls return HTTP status codes and response bodies.

[[axe_console]]
{axe_console}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing direct terminal invocation.
Standard output and standard error are visible to the user.
Output can be routed directly to stdout/stderr.

[[axe_mcp_transport]]
{axe_mcp_transport}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing invocation through the MCP (Model Context Protocol) transport layer.
Standard error is swallowed by the transport — all output must be captured in a return buffer to reach the caller.

[[axe_daemon_runtime]]
{axe_daemon_runtime}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a long-running system process.
Errors are logged, not displayed.
Fatal means graceful shutdown with cleanup.
No human in immediate feedback loop.

== Axial Operation Terms

[[axo_procedure]]
{axo_procedure}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing an executable operation with defined inputs, behavior, outputs, and completion semantics.
Standalone operations not affiliated with an entity.
Voiced at the
{axl_definition_site}
via
{axvo_procedure}.

[[axo_role]]
{axo_role}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing an operational identity pattern that defines capabilities and permissions.
Determines available operations and access boundaries.

[[axo_identity]]
{axo_identity}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing the abstract concept of an operational identity.
An entity capable of authentication and holding authorities within a system.
{axo_identity}
is the pattern;
{axo_actor_s}
are named instantiations with specific bindings.

[[axo_actor]]
{axo_actor}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a specific named identity with defined responsibilities and exclusive authority over an operational domain.
{axo_actor_s}
instantiate
{axo_identity}
with concrete bindings (human via OAuth, service via service account) and permissions.

[[axo_entity]]
{axo_entity}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing an object with both structure and behavior.
Unlike
{axr_record}
which captures pure data,
{axo_entity}
includes methods, builder patterns, or lifecycle operations.
{axl_voicing_s}
describe the entity's data members and its behavioral interface (construction, mutation, registration).
Typical manifestations include Rust structs with `impl` blocks, builder-pattern APIs, or objects that participate in registration protocols.

== Axial Type Terms

[[axt_type]]
{axt_type}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a data format constraint that defines valid values and structures.
Enforces data validation and enables type-safe operations.

[[axt_string]]
{axt_string}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing text data type with optional pattern validation.
{axl_voicing_s}
may address context-specific concerns including:
+
* Encoding (ASCII, UTF-8, locale-dependent character sets)
* Termination semantics (null-terminated, length-prefixed, delimiter-bounded)
* Mutability (immutable values, mutable buffers)
* Memory management (owned, borrowed, reference-counted)
* Safety constraints (shell-safe, URL-safe, JSON-safe, SQL-safe)
* Format patterns (timestamps, identifiers, hashes, paths)
* Size and bounds (fixed-length, maximum length, unbounded)

[[axt_number]]
{axt_number}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing numeric value type supporting integers and floating point.
May specify range constraints or precision requirements.

[[axt_boolean]]
{axt_boolean}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing binary true/false value type.
Maps to language-specific boolean implementations.

[[axt_array]]
{axt_array}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing ordered collection of typed elements.
Specifies element type and optional size constraints.

[[axt_object]]
{axt_object}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing named field collection forming a composite type.
Contains typed fields with defined cardinality.

[[axt_map]]
{axt_map}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a dictionary structure with typed keys and typed values.
2-arity: reads two
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
from following text — the first identifies the key type, the second identifies the value type.
Keys are unique within the map; values are accessed by key lookup.
Serialization format determines key ordering guarantees and key type constraints (e.g., JSON restricts keys to strings).

[[axt_enumeration]]
{axt_enumeration}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing constrained set of valid string values.
Restricts values to predefined options.

[[axt_enum_value]]
{axt_enum_value}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing single allowed option within an
{axt_enumeration}.
Must be explicitly listed in enumeration definition.
+
Encoding is carried by an
{axd_dimension}
on the voicing line — e.g.,
{axd_string}
for string-token-encoded values.
The encoding
{axd_dimension}
specifies the
{mcm_sprue}
arity for the wire-level value.
Exactly one encoding dimension must qualify every
{axt_enum_value}
voicing whose
{axt_enumeration}
participates in a wire form.

[[axt_insignia]]
{axt_insignia}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a glyph-prefixed typed identity string with seed-based allocation.
Extends
{axt_string}
with structural properties: a unicode verification glyph (decorative in input, mandatory in output), a fixed encoding format, a monotonic seed allocation mechanism, and optional hierarchical embedding (parent identity encoded as a prefix substring).
+
Every
{axt_insignia}
declares an encoding nature via
{axd_dimension_s}:
{axd_algebraic}
or
{axd_temporal}.

[[axt_variant]]
{axt_variant}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a value that can take one of several distinct forms, where each form may have different structure.
Unlike
{axt_enumeration}
which constrains to predefined strings,
{axt_variant}
allows structurally different alternatives.
{axl_voicing_s}
define the specific forms and discrimination logic for their context.

== Axial Record Terms

{axr_record_s}
and
{axr_member_s}
provide format-agnostic patterns for structured data.
Format-specific variants (JSON, protobuf, XML, etc.) capture serialization-specific semantics.
{axr_member_s}
inherit format context from their parent
{axr_record}.

[[axr_record]]
{axr_record}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a named collection of typed
{axr_member_s}.
Abstract pattern that format-specific variants voice with serialization semantics.
Use format-specific variants (
{axr_record_json},
{axr_record_protobuf},
etc.) when format is known.

[[axr_record_json]]
{axr_record_json}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a JSON object structure.
{axr_member_s}
are unordered, string-keyed properties.
Requires
{axd_nullable}
specification since JSON has explicit null.

[[axr_record_protobuf]]
{axr_record_protobuf}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a protobuf message.
{axr_member_s}
require
{axd_field_number}
and
{axd_wire_type}
dimensions.
Supports schema evolution through field numbering.

[[axr_record_xml]]
{axr_record_xml}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing an XML element structure.
{axr_member_s}
require
{axd_xml_form}
to distinguish attributes from child elements.
Order may be significant depending on schema.

[[axr_record_yaml]]
{axr_record_yaml}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a YAML mapping.
Superset of JSON with anchors, aliases, and multi-document support.
{axr_member_s}
follow JSON-like semantics with extended syntax.

[[axr_record_binary]]
{axr_record_binary}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a binary/memory structure.
{axr_member_s}
require
{axd_endianness}
for multi-byte fields.
Fixed layout with explicit size and alignment.

[[axr_member]]
{axr_member}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a typed element within an
{axr_record}.
Inherits serialization format from parent
{axr_record}.
Default 1-arity: reads one
{mcm_sprue}
from following text — the serialized property name (e.g., the JSON key).
The
{mcm_sprue}
is the exact wire-level name; the
{mcm_quoin}
is the conceptual name.
Additional type motifs on the annotation line (e.g.,
{axt_map},
{axt_string})
contribute their own arity to the total read sequence:
{axr_member}
sprue first, then type motif lookaheads.
Qualified by
{axd_dimension_s}
in
{axl_annotation}:
`//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axt_string`
+
Under
{axr_record_json},
exactly one of
{axd_property}
or
{axd_positional}
must qualify the
{axr_member}.
{axd_property}
preserves the 1-arity sprue contract.
{axd_positional}
suppresses it (0-arity for sprue) since the member's value provides the parent map's key directly and no separate sprue exists in the wire form.

== Axial JSON Terms

[[axj_structure]]
{axj_structure}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing named schema definition for JSON objects.
Defines complete object shape with field specifications.

[[axj_field]]
{axj_field}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing named, typed element within a
{axj_structure}.
Combines tag identifier with type constraint.

[[axj_field_tag]]
{axj_field_tag}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing the key identifier for a
{axj_field}.
Follows naming conventions of target specification.

[[axj_field_type]]
{axj_field_type}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing the value constraint for a
{axj_field}.
Determines parsing and validation behavior.

[[axj_array_element]]
{axj_array_element}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing type constraint for members of an
{axt_array}
field.
Applies uniformly to all array elements.

[[axj_nested_field]]
{axj_nested_field}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing fields within object-typed fields enabling hierarchy.
Maintains type safety through nested validation.

[[axj_root_structure]]
{axj_root_structure}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing top-level JSON object schema.
Entry point for JSON document validation.

[[axj_field_cardinality]]
{axj_field_cardinality}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing required, optional, or repeated nature of a field.
Maps to validation rules in implementation.

[[axj_type_reference]]
{axj_type_reference}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing pointer to another
{axj_structure}
for composition.
Maintains referential integrity across definitions.

== Axial Argument Terms

[[axa_argument]]
{axa_argument}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing command parameter accepting a value.
Foundation for CLI and function interfaces.

[[axa_positional]]
{axa_positional}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing order-determines-meaning parameter in command invocation.
Position in argument list defines semantic role.

[[axa_keyword]]
{axa_keyword}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing name-determines-meaning parameter with explicit identifier.
Order-independent with explicit naming.

[[axa_long_option]]
{axa_long_option}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing keyword argument with `--` prefix following GNU conventions.
Uses hyphenated multi-word names.

[[axa_short_option]]
{axa_short_option}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing keyword argument with single `-` prefix for brevity.
Often provides shorthand for
{axa_long_option}.

[[axa_option_name]]
{axa_option_name}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing the identifier portion of a keyword argument.
Defines the semantic meaning of the option.

[[axa_option_value]]
{axa_option_value}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing the data portion following an option identifier.
Subject to
{axa_argument_type}
constraints.

[[axa_argument_type]]
{axa_argument_type}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing expected data format for an
{axa_argument}.
Enforces type safety at interface boundary.

[[axa_required]]
{axa_required}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing attribute indicating an
{axa_argument}
must be provided.
Absence triggers
{axc_fatal}
or error response.

[[axa_optional]]
{axa_optional}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing attribute indicating an
{axa_argument}
may be omitted.
Provides flexibility in interface usage.

[[axa_defaulted]]
{axa_defaulted}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing attribute indicating an
{axa_argument}
has a fallback value.
Specifies behavior when
{axa_optional}
argument omitted.

[[axa_argument_list]]
{axa_argument_list}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a structured section documenting an operation's arguments.
{axl_voices}
{axhopt_typed_parameter}
/{axhopa_arg_parameter}
in CLI context.
Within an
{axa_argument_list},
{axd_dimension}
annotations may precede bullet items to qualify argument usage without requiring anchors.
Each item references a globally-defined argument term.
The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
+
Example section header definition:
+
[source,asciidoc]
----
[[jjds_arguments]]
//axl_voices axa_argument_list
{jjds_arguments}::
Section header introducing operation arguments.
----
+
Example usage in an operation:
+
[source,asciidoc]
----
{jjds_arguments}

//axd_required
* {jjda_silks}

//axd_optional axd_defaulted
* {jjda_file}
----

[[axa_cli_option]]
{axa_cli_option}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a command-line option definition.
{axl_voicing_s}
require the definition text to specify:
+
* The long form (e.g., `--file`)
* The short form, or its absence (e.g., `-f` or "none")
+
Example:
+
[source,asciidoc]
----
[[jjda_file]]
//axl_voices axa_cli_option
{jjda_file}::
Path to the Gallops JSON file.
Long: `--file`.
Short: none.
Default: `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
----

[[axa_exit_uniform]]
{axa_exit_uniform}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing an Exit Status section that follows standard Unix conventions.
{axl_voices}
{axhoc_completion}
in CLI context.
Exit 0 indicates success; any non-zero exit indicates error.
{axl_voicing_s}
may use terse notation since the pattern is conventional.
The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
Specifications define separate section header terms for uniform vs enumerated exit patterns.
+
Example section header definition:
+
[source,asciidoc]
----
[[jjds_exit_uniform]]
//axl_voices axa_exit_uniform
{jjds_exit_uniform}::
Section header for operations with standard exit semantics.
----
+
Example usage:
+
[source,asciidoc]
----
{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
----

[[axa_exit_enumerated]]
{axa_exit_enumerated}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing an Exit Status section with distinct exit codes carrying specific meanings.
{axl_voices}
{axhoc_completion}
in CLI context.
{axl_voicing_s}
require the definition text to list each exit code and its meaning.
The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
Specifications define separate section header terms for uniform vs enumerated exit patterns.
+
Example section header definition:
+
[source,asciidoc]
----
[[jjds_exit_enumerated]]
//axl_voices axa_exit_enumerated
{jjds_exit_enumerated}::
Section header for operations with distinct exit codes.
----
+
Example usage:
+
[source,asciidoc]
----
{jjds_exit_enumerated}

* 0 — found and valid
* 1 — not found
* 2 — found but malformed
----

== Axial Interface Terms

[[axi_command_interface]]
{axi_command_interface}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing complete specification of a command's invocation pattern.
Defines syntax and semantics for command usage.

[[axi_subcommand]]
{axi_subcommand}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing nested command within parent command structure.
Inherits context from parent command.

[[axi_command_signature]]
{axi_command_signature}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing complete parameter specification for a command.
Serves as contract for command invocation.

[[axi_endpoint]]
{axi_endpoint}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing URL path accepting HTTP requests.
Defines resource location and accepted operations.

[[axi_api_method]]
{axi_api_method}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing HTTP verb combined with
{axi_endpoint}
specification.
Includes request and response specifications.

[[axi_url_parameter]]
{axi_url_parameter}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing query string key-value pair in URL.
Appears after `?` in URL structure.

[[axi_hash_parameter]]
{axi_hash_parameter}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing fragment identifier for client-side state.
Persists state without server round-trips.

[[axi_request_body]]
{axi_request_body}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing structured input payload for HTTP requests.
Validated against
{axj_structure}
definitions.

[[axi_response_body]]
{axi_response_body}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing structured output payload from HTTP responses.
Conforms to documented
{axj_structure}.

[[axi_cli_interface]]
{axi_cli_interface}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing command-line invocation pattern specification.
Documents
{axa_argument_s}
and behavior.

[[axi_cli_program]]
{axi_cli_program}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Unix-style command-line program.
Invoked from a shell with arguments.
Writes results to stdout, errors to stderr.
Returns exit status: 0 for success, non-zero for failure.
Suitable for scripted, non-interactive use.

[[axi_cli_command_group]]
{axi_cli_command_group}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a namespace within a CLI that contains subcommands.
Does not perform operations itself; serves as organizational container.
May be nested within an
{axi_cli_program}
or another
{axi_cli_command_group}.
Inherits Unix CLI semantics from parent.

[[axi_cli_subcommand]]
{axi_cli_subcommand}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a leaf operation within an
{axi_cli_program}
or
{axi_cli_command_group}.
Performs an actual operation; not a container for other commands.
Inherits Unix CLI semantics from parent: arguments, stdout, stderr, exit status.

[[axi_rest_api]]
{axi_rest_api}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing resource-based web service interface.
Maps operations to HTTP methods and resources.

[[axi_rpc_interface]]
{axi_rpc_interface}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing remote procedure call pattern for service invocation.
Emphasizes operation semantics over HTTP methods.

[[axi_sse_endpoint]]
{axi_sse_endpoint}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing server-sent event stream for push notifications.
Enables real-time updates to connected clients.

[[axi_cc_slash_command]]
{axi_cc_slash_command}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Claude Code slash command skill artifact.
Implemented as a `.claude/commands/*.md` file containing orchestration logic that the LLM executes as a skill.
Slash commands coordinate multiple
{axi_cli_subcommand}
invocations, gather context, and apply workflow policies.

[[axi_cc_claudemd_verb]]
{axi_cc_claudemd_verb}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing an action verb defined in a
{axi_cc_claudemd_section}
that maps user intent to tool-layer operations.
The verb exists as a concept in the LLM context — either mapping directly to a single
{axi_cli_subcommand}
(direct verb) or orchestrating multiple operations (multistep verb implemented by a
{axi_cc_slash_command}).

[[axi_cc_claudemd_section]]
{axi_cc_claudemd_section}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a managed section within a project CLAUDE.md file.
Provides persistent LLM context that bridges specification-layer concepts to runtime execution.
Typically contains command references, vocabulary mappings, and operational guidance.

[[axi_protocol]]
{axi_protocol}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing the bilateral discipline binding two
{axo_actor_s}
— a caller and a callee — through a defined sequence of call and return envelopes.
Distinct from
{axi_rpc_interface}
which describes one side's view of a service;
{axi_protocol}
is the relationship itself, owning transport, lifecycle, and concurrency presumptions.
A protocol quoin carries transport via
{axd_dimension_s}
(e.g.,
{axd_file_bus}),
lifecycle presumptions (e.g.,
{axd_disjoint_lifecycle}),
and concurrency discipline as dimensions on the primary voicing.
Structural detail — the roster of transactions, call/return envelopes, and member types — lives at the
{axl_detail_site}
under
{axhpb_protocol}
and nested hierarchy markers.

content.content:
1435	== Axial Dimension Terms
1436	
1437	{axd_dimension_s}
1438	are modifiers that appear in
1439	{axl_annotation_s}
1440	to specify characteristics of members and arguments.
1441	Unlike primary
1442	{axl_motif_s},
1443	{axd_dimension_s}
1444	do not stand alone but qualify how a term voices its primary motif.
1445	Applicability of
1446	{axd_dimension_s}
1447	depends on serialization format context.
1448	
1449	[[axd_dimension]]
1450	{axd_dimension}::
1451	A characteristic modifier that qualifies a member or argument in an
1452	{axl_annotation}.
1453	{axd_dimension_s}
1454	appear after the primary
1455	{axl_motif}
1456	in annotation format: `//axl_voices primary dimension ...`
1457	
1458	=== Value Dimensions
1459	
1460	These
1461	{axd_dimension_s}
1462	describe value characteristics and apply across most serialization formats.
1463	They encompass both presence semantics (required, optional, defaulted) and structural semantics (repeated, list).
1464	
1465	[[axd_required]]
1466	{axd_required}::
1467	Member or argument must be present.
1468	Absence triggers validation error or
1469	{axc_fatal}.
1470	
1471	[[axd_optional]]
1472	{axd_optional}::
1473	Member or argument may be omitted.
1474	Absence is valid and handled gracefully.
1475	
1476	[[axd_nullable]]
1477	{axd_nullable}::
1478	Null, nil, or None is a valid value, distinct from absent.
1479	Required for JSON where null is an explicit value type.
1480	Some formats (protobuf) conflate nullable with optional.
1481	
1482	[[axd_none]]
1483	{axd_none}::
1484	Section or field is explicitly declared as having no content.
1485	Distinguishes intentional absence from omission.
1486	Use in section annotations to mark compliance with required sections that have no applicable content.
1487	
1488	[[axd_defaulted]]
1489	{axd_defaulted}::
1490	Has fallback value when absent.
1491	Voicing should specify the default value in prose.
1492	
1493	[[axd_repeated]]
1494	{axd_repeated}::
1495	May have multiple values (array, list, repeated field).
1496	Absence typically means empty collection, not error.
1497	Applies to usage sites (arguments, members) where multiple occurrences are permitted.
1498	
1499	[[axd_list]]
1500	{axd_list}::
1501	Single value containing delimited multiple values.
1502	Distinct from
1503	{axd_repeated}
1504	which indicates multiple separate occurrences.
1505	Applies to type voicings where the type intrinsically represents a delimited collection.
1506	Voicing should specify the delimiter in prose (space-delimited, comma-delimited, etc.).
1507	
1508	=== Format-Specific Dimensions
1509	
1510	These
1511	{axd_dimension_s}
1512	apply only to specific serialization formats.
1513	Using them in forbidden contexts is a type-scheme error.
1514	
1515	[[axd_field_number]]
1516	{axd_field_number}::
1517	Numeric field identifier for protobuf encoding.
1518	Required for protobuf format.
1519	Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.
1520	
1521	[[axd_wire_type]]
1522	{axd_wire_type}::
1523	Protobuf encoding type (varint, fixed32, fixed64, length-delimited).
1524	Required for protobuf format.
1525	Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.
1526	
1527	[[axd_endianness]]
1528	{axd_endianness}::
1529	Byte order for binary encoding (big-endian, little-endian).
1530	Required for binary format.
1531	Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, protobuf.
1532	
1533	[[axd_xml_form]]
1534	{axd_xml_form}::
1535	Whether XML member is attribute or child element.
1536	Required for XML format.
1537	Forbidden for JSON, YAML, protobuf, binary.
1538	
1539	[[axd_property]]
1540	{axd_property}::
1541	JSON
1542	{axr_member}
1543	encoded as a named property within its containing
1544	{axr_record_json}.
1545	The wire-level key is a fixed sprue declared at the definition site;
1546	the 1-arity sprue lookahead applies and is fulfilled by a backtick-delimited
1547	{mcm_sprue}
1548	in the following text.
1549	Required for JSON
1550	{axr_member_s}
1551	whose value sits at a named slot.
1552	Forbidden for protobuf, XML, YAML, binary.
1553	
1554	[[axd_positional]]
1555	{axd_positional}::
1556	JSON
1557	{axr_member}
1558	encoded by structural position rather than by a named property.
1559	The member's value provides the parent map's key directly;
1560	no separate sprue exists in the wire form.
1561	Suppresses the 1-arity sprue lookahead — under
1562	{axd_positional},
1563	{axr_member}
1564	becomes 0-arity for sprue.
1565	Required for JSON
1566	{axr_member_s}
1567	whose value is the parent map's key (e.g., a record's identity field encoded as the map entry's key).
1568	Forbidden for protobuf, XML, YAML, binary.
1569	
1570	Exactly one of
1571	{axd_property}
1572	or
1573	{axd_positional}
1574	must be present on every
1575	{axr_member}
1576	under an
1577	{axr_record_json}
1578	carrier whose value participates in the JSON wire form.
1579	Members that are computed, ephemeral, or stored outside the JSON wire are not
1580	{axr_member_s}
1581	of the
1582	{axr_record_json}
1583	and should not carry the
1584	{axr_member}
1585	voicing in the first place.
1586	
1587	[[axd_string]]
1588	{axd_string}::
1589	{axt_enum_value}
1590	encoded as a literal string token in the wire form.
1591	Carries a 1-arity sprue lookahead, fulfilled by a backtick-delimited
1592	{mcm_sprue}
1593	in the following text — the literal wire-level value as it appears in the carrier.
1594	The
1595	{mcm_sprue}
1596	is the exact wire-name; the
1597	{mcm_quoin}
1598	is the conceptual enum-value identity.
1599	Carrier-agnostic: applies wherever an
1600	{axt_enumeration}
1601	serializes its values as string tokens (JSON strings, bash regime values, YAML scalars, env-var values).
1602	Forbidden when the wire form is non-string (numeric, bitmask, object-tagged, positional, untagged variant) — those encodings will mint companion dimensions.
1603	
1604	=== Lifecycle Dimensions
1605	
1606	These
1607	{axd_dimension_s}
1608	qualify
1609	{axo_procedure}
1610	voicings to specify execution lifecycle.
1611	Exactly one lifecycle dimension is required for any
1612	{axo_procedure}
1613	voicing.
1614	Lifecycle dimensions are mutually exclusive.
1615	
1616	[[axd_transient]]
1617	{axd_transient}::
1618	{axo_procedure}
1619	executes and completes.
1620	The default lifecycle for most procedures.
1621	{axhoc_completion}
1622	documents exit status or return value.
1623	
1624	[[axd_longrunning]]
1625	{axd_longrunning}::
1626	{axo_procedure}
1627	runs continuously until explicitly stopped.
1628	{axhoc_completion}
1629	documents startup success criteria, not termination.
1630	Requires
1631	{axhos_step} (startup)
1632	and
1633	{axhos_step} (shutdown)
1634	sections.
1635	May include
1636	{axhos_step} (health, signals, recovery)
1637	sections.
1638	
1639	[[axd_periodic]]
1640	{axd_periodic}::
1641	{axo_procedure}
1642	executes on a schedule.
1643	{axhoc_completion}
1644	applies per-run.
1645	Requires
1646	{axhos_step} (schedule)
1647	section.
1648	May include
1649	{axhos_step} (idempotency, overlap)
1650	sections.
1651	
1652	=== Regime Dimensions
1653	
1654	These
1655	{axd_dimension_s}
1656	qualify regime structure annotations.
1657	
1658	[[axd_conditional]]
1659	{axd_conditional}::
1660	Group activation depends on one or more gate conditions.
1661	A group annotated with
1662	{axd_conditional}
1663	requires corresponding hierarchy markers in the subdocument to include at least one
1664	{axhrgc_gate}
1665	specifying the activation criteria.
1666	
1667	[[axrd_singleton]]
1668	{axrd_singleton}::
1669	Regime has exactly one assignment source.
1670	No enumeration or cross-instance operations apply.
1671	
1672	[[axrd_manifold]]
1673	{axrd_manifold}::
1674	Regime has multiple assignment sources (one per instance).
1675	Enumeration and cross-instance operations are meaningful.
1676	
1677	=== Protocol Dimensions
1678	
1679	These
1680	{axd_dimension_s}
1681	qualify
1682	{axi_protocol}
1683	voicings to specify transport, lifecycle, and concurrency presumptions of the bilateral discipline.
1684	
1685	[[axd_file_bus]]
1686	{axd_file_bus}::
1687	Transport dimension on an
1688	{axi_protocol}:
1689	participants communicate via a shared filesystem directory acting as the wire.
1690	The caller writes call envelopes as files; the callee observes atomic file appearance via rename(2) (write-temp-then-rename) and writes return envelopes the same way.
1691	No sockets, no in-process shared memory — POSIX file I/O is the entire wire.
1692	+
1693	*Index discipline:* participants coordinate transaction ordering via monotonic indices assigned by the caller and embedded in filenames or envelope bodies.
1694	Indices are session-local and reset on callee restart; the caller detects restart via index-collision or an explicit readiness exchange.
1695	Filename conventions for call and return envelopes are declared in the voicing spec, not in this motif.
1696	+
1697	*Atomicity:* every envelope write uses the rename(2) idiom so receivers never observe partial content.
1698	+
1699	*Wire-format neutrality:*
1700	{axd_file_bus}
1701	is transport-only; envelope contents are described by separate
1702	{axr_record_s}
1703	voicings (e.g.,
1704	{axr_record_json}).
1705	
1706	[[axd_disjoint_lifecycle]]
1707	{axd_disjoint_lifecycle}::
1708	Lifecycle dimension on an
1709	{axi_protocol}:
1710	the caller and callee have independent start and stop lifecycles.
1711	Neither actor can assume the other exists when it itself begins;
1712	each call must handle the possibility that the peer is absent, starting, or no longer the same instance that handled the previous call.
1713	+
1714	Implies the caller must observe callee presence before committing to a transaction, and must gracefully degrade or retry when the callee is unavailable.
1715	The specific observation mechanism (readiness probe, file-presence check, timeout) is declared in the protocol's detail site, not in this motif.
1716	+
1717	Contrasts with shared-session protocols where caller and callee share a bounded lifetime (e.g., an HTTP request-response pair scoped to a single process).
1718	
1719	=== Dimension Applicability
1720	
1721	Per-format guidance for
1722	{axd_dimension}
1723	usage:
1724	
1725	[cols="<,^,^,^,^,^",options="header"]
1726	|===
1727	|Dimension |JSON |Protobuf |XML |Binary |YAML
1728	
1729	|{axd_required}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
1730	|{axd_optional}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
1731	|{axd_nullable}     |required  |implicit  |recommend |context   |recommend
1732	|{axd_defaulted}    |optional  |required  |optional  |optional  |optional
1733	|{axd_repeated}     |recommend |required  |recommend |recommend |recommend
1734	|{axd_list}         |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional
1735	|{axd_field_number} |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
1736	|{axd_wire_type}    |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
1737	|{axd_endianness}   |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden
1738	|{axd_xml_form}     |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden
1739	|===
1740	
1741	Legend: required = must specify; recommend = should specify; optional = may specify; implicit = format handles; context = depends on usage; forbidden = not applicable.
1742	
1743	== Axial Format Terms
1744	
1745	{axf_format_s}
1746	specify how configuration data is serialized and carried.
1747	{axrg_assignment_s}
1748	use a particular
1749	{axf_format}
1750	to encode
1751	{axrg_variable}
1752	values in files that can be sourced, included, or parsed.
1753	
1754	[[axf_format]]
1755	{axf_format}::
1756	A
1757	{axl_motif}
1758	representing a serialization encoding for configuration data.
1759	{axf_format_s}
1760	determine how
1761	{axrg_assignment_s}
1762	are written to and read from files.
1763	
1764	[[axf_bash]]
1765	{axf_bash}::
1766	A
1767	{axl_motif}
1768	representing bash script format for configuration.
1769	Files are source-able via `. file` or `source file`.
1770	Variables declared as `VAR=value` without export, or `export VAR=value` for environment propagation.
1771	
1772	[[axf_makefile]]
1773	{axf_makefile}::
1774	A
1775	{axl_motif}
1776	representing makefile format for configuration.
1777	Files are includable via `include file`.
1778	Variables declared as `VAR := value` for immediate expansion or `VAR = value` for deferred expansion.
1779	
1780	[[axf_json]]
1781	{axf_json}::
1782	A
1783	{axl_motif}
1784	representing JSON format for configuration.
1785	Standard JSON object structure with string keys mapping to typed values.
1786	Parsed by standard JSON libraries.
1787	
1788	[[axf_yaml]]
1789	{axf_yaml}::
1790	A
1791	{axl_motif}
1792	representing YAML format for configuration.
1793	Superset of JSON with additional syntax for readability.
1794	Parsed by YAML libraries with attention to version-specific semantics.
1795	
1796	== Axial Regime Terms
1797	
1798	{axrg_regime_s}
1799	are structured configuration systems that coordinate specification, validation, and rendering of typed configuration data.
1800	
1801	[[axrg_regime]]
1802	{axrg_regime}::
1803	A
1804	{axl_motif}
1805	representing a structured configuration system.
1806	A
1807	{axrg_regime}
1808	binds related
1809	{axrg_variable_s}
1810	under a namespace prefix, with coordinated specification, validation, and rendering components.
1811	The specification defines what can be configured;
1812	{axrg_assignment_s}
1813	provide specific values.
1814	
1815	[[axrg_variable]]
1816	{axrg_variable}::
1817	A
1818	{axl_motif}
1819	representing a named configuration element at schema level.
1820	Each
1821	{axrg_variable}
1822	belongs to an
1823	{axrg_regime}
1824	and defines a name, type constraints, and relationships with other variables.
1825	{axrg_variable_s}
1826	are the "what can be configured" definitions;
1827	{axrg_assignment_s}
1828	bind values to them.
1829	
1830	[[axrg_assignment]]
1831	{axrg_assignment}::
1832	A
1833	{axl_motif}
1834	representing a specific value bound to an
1835	{axrg_variable}.
1836	A single binding — one variable, one value.
1837	{axrg_assignment_s}
1838	are conveyed by an
1839	{axrg_carrier},
1840	whose concrete form and
1841	{axf_format}
1842	depend on the regime's provenance.
1843	
1844	[[axrg_carrier]]
1845	{axrg_carrier}::
1846	A
1847	{axl_motif}
1848	representing the artifact holding one instance's complete set of
1849	{axrg_assignment_s}
1850	for a single
1851	{axrg_regime}.
1852	The carrier's concrete form is selected by its provenance
1853	{axd_dimension}:
1854	{axrd_file_sourced}
1855	manifests as a file at a path (encoding via
1856	{axf_format});
1857	{axrd_constructed}
1858	as values assembled by a defining module and sourced into consumers;
1859	{axrd_env_inherited}
1860	as an inherited process environment scope.
1861	A
1862	{axrd_singleton}
1863	regime has exactly one carrier;
1864	a
1865	{axrd_manifold}
1866	regime has one carrier per instance.
1867	Only a
1868	{axrd_file_sourced}
1869	carrier has a path locus: the singleton case resolves to a single path, the manifold case to one templated path per instance.
1870	Whether that locus earns a dedicated quoin or is composed in prose from a root quoin plus a literal leaf is a per-specification judgment, not a property of the motif.
1871	Virtual carriers
1872	({axrd_constructed},
1873	{axrd_env_inherited})
1874	have no consumer-facing path.
1875	
1876	[[axrg_prefix]]
1877	{axrg_prefix}::
1878	A
1879	{axl_motif}
1880	representing the stable character sequence that namespaces all
1881	{axrg_variable_s}
1882	within an
1883	{axrg_regime}.
1884	Typically composed of a project marker and regime marker (e.g., `RBRR_` = `RB` + `RR` + `_`).
1885	Once established, an
1886	{axrg_prefix}
1887	becomes deeply embedded across code, configuration files, and documentation.
1888	Changing an
1889	{axrg_prefix}
1890	requires coordinated refactoring across all consuming artifacts.
1891	
1892	== Axial Regime Provenance Dimensions
1893	
1894	Provenance dimensions qualify an
1895	{axrg_carrier}
1896	to declare its concrete form and how its
1897	{axrg_assignment_s}
1898	arrive at
1899	{axvr_kindle}
1900	time.
1901	They are declared on
1902	{axvr_regime}
1903	to state which provenance(s) a regime's carrier supports.
1904	
1905	[[axrd_file_sourced]]
1906	{axrd_file_sourced}::
1907	Assignments loaded from a persistent file via source or equivalent mechanism.
1908	The file path is known at load time and the regime module is responsible for locating and sourcing it.
1909	
1910	[[axrd_env_inherited]]
1911	{axrd_env_inherited}::
1912	Assignments received as environment variables from a parent process.
1913	The regime module finds variables already in scope; no file sourcing occurs.
1914	
1915	[[axrd_constructed]]
1916	{axrd_constructed}::
1917	Assignments computed or set programmatically before kindle.
1918	Values are assembled by application logic rather than loaded from a static source.
1919	
1920	== Axial Regime Annotation Terms
1921	
1922	Regime annotations use the prefix-discriminated form (`//ax...` with no space after `//`) to provide structured metadata about regime definitions in AsciiDoc specifications.
1923	Two complementary annotation families enable regime specification across parent documents and their subdocuments.
1924	
1925	=== Definition-Site Voicing Annotations (axvr_*)
1926	
1927	{axvr_*} annotations appear in parent documents between anchor and definition, declaring the regime role of the term being defined.
1928	The surface syntax follows the prefix-discriminated pattern:
1929	
1930	[source,asciidoc]
1931	----
1932	[[rbrv_sigil]]
1933	//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_xname
1934	{rbrv_sigil}::
1935	Vessel identifier for build configurations.
1936	----
1937	
1938	The annotation line starts with `//axvr_` (no space after `//`), followed by the specific voicing term, then space-separated dimension and type terms.
1939	The anchor immediately preceding the annotation must match the first
1940	{mcm_attribute_reference}
1941	in the definition text that follows.
1942	
1943	[[axvr_regime]]
1944	{axvr_regime}::
1945	Voices a regime definition.
1946	The annotated term defines a structured configuration system that binds related
1947	{axrg_variable_s}
1948	under a namespace prefix.
1949	+
1950	{axd_dimension_s}:
1951	{axf_format}
1952	(specifying how
1953	{axrg_assignment_s}
1954	are carried).
1955	
1956	[[axvr_variable]]
1957	{axvr_variable}::
1958	Voices a regime variable definition.
1959	The annotated term defines a named configuration element within a regime.
1960	The definition text must include, as its second
1961	{mcm_attribute_reference},
1962	the parent regime (an anchor that voices
1963	{axrg_regime}).
1964	This ties each variable unequivocally to its regime.
1965	+
1966	{axd_dimension_s}:
1967	{axd_required}
1968	or
1969	{axd_optional},
1970	type
1971	{axl_motif}
1972	(base type or type voicing).
1973	
1974	[[axvr_group]]
1975	{axvr_group}::
1976	Voices a regime group definition.
1977	The annotated term defines a named grouping of related
1978	{axrg_variable_s}
1979	within a regime.
1980	The definition text must include, as its second
1981	{mcm_attribute_reference},
1982	the parent regime (an anchor that voices
1983	{axrg_regime}).
1984	+
1985	{axd_dimension_s}:
1986	{axd_conditional}
1987	(group activation depends on gate conditions in subdocument).
1988	
1989	[[axvr_kindle]]
1990	{axvr_kindle}::
1991	Voices a regime kindle operation definition.
1992	The annotated term is a dedicated
1993	{mcm_quoin}
1994	naming the silent initialization phase for exactly one
1995	{axrg_regime}:
1996	setting defaults, detecting unexpected variables, and building derived structures.
1997	
1998	[[axvr_validate]]
1999	{axvr_validate}::
2000	Voices a regime validate operation definition.
2001	The annotated term is a dedicated
2002	{mcm_quoin}
2003	naming the strict verification phase for exactly one
2004	{axrg_regime}:
2005	checking all type and constraint requirements, failing on first violation.
2006	
2007	[[axvr_render]]
2008	{axvr_render}::
2009	Voices a regime render operation definition.
2010	The annotated term is a dedicated
2011	{mcm_quoin}
2012	naming the diagnostic display phase for exactly one
2013	{axrg_regime}:
2014	producing human-readable presentation of current state.
2015	Rendering occurs before validation so all fields are visible on failure.
2016	
2017	[[axvr_list]]
2018	{axvr_list}::
2019	Voices a regime list operation definition.
2020	The annotated term is a dedicated
2021	{mcm_quoin}
2022	naming the enumeration operation for exactly one
2023	{axrd_manifold}
2024	{axrg_regime}:
2025	listing available instances.
2026	
2027	[[axvr_survey]]
2028	{axvr_survey}::
2029	Voices a regime survey operation definition.
2030	The annotated term is a dedicated
2031	{mcm_quoin}
2032	naming the read-only cross-instance summary operation for exactly one
2033	{axrd_manifold}
2034	{axrg_regime}.
2035	
2036	[[axvr_audit]]
2037	{axvr_audit}::
2038	Voices a regime audit operation definition.
2039	The annotated term is a dedicated
2040	{mcm_quoin}
2041	naming the cross-instance validation plus external verification operation for exactly one
2042	{axrd_manifold}
2043	{axrg_regime}.
2044	
2045	=== Standalone Hierarchy Markers (axhr*_)
2046	
2047	Hierarchy markers appear in regime subdocuments as standalone annotations, NOT between anchor and definition.
2048	Each marker uses the prefix-discriminated form and reads ahead a specified number of
2049	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
2050	from the following text to establish hierarchical context.
2051	
2052	The surface syntax:
2053	
2054	[source,asciidoc]
2055	----
2056	//axhrb_regime
2057	{rbrv_regime}
2058	
2059	//axhrgv_variable
2060	{rbrv_sigil}
2061	----
2062	
2063	The hierarchy marker line starts with `//axhr` (no space after `//`), followed by the sub-prefix identifying the marker type.
2064	Following text contains the
2065	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
2066	that the marker reads ahead.
2067	
2068	==== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities
2069	
2070	[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
2071	|===
2072	|Marker |Lookahead |Reads
2073	
2074	|{axhrb_regime}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The regime this subdoc describes
2075	|{axhrv_variable}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |An ungrouped regime variable
2076	|{axhrgb_group}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A group (must match {axvr_group} in parent)
2077	|{axhrgc_gate}      |2 {mcm_attribute_reference_s}|Enumerated variable + enum value
2078	|{axhrgv_variable}  |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A variable within current group
2079	|{axhro_kindle}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level kindle operation for this regime
2080	|{axhro_validate}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level validate operation for this regime
2081	|{axhro_render}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level render operation for this regime
2082	|{axhro_list}       |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level list operation for this regime
2083	|{axhro_survey}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level survey operation for this regime
2084	|{axhro_audit}      |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level audit operation for this regime
2085	|===
2086	
2087	[[axhrb_regime]]
2088	{axhrb_regime}::
2089	Marks the regime that a subdocument describes.
2090	Lookahead: 1
2091	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2092	— the regime this subdoc describes.
2093	The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
2094	{axvr_regime}
2095	in the parent document.
2096	
2097	[[axhrv_variable]]
2098	{axhrv_variable}::
2099	Marks an ungrouped regime variable in a subdocument.
2100	Lookahead: 1
2101	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2102	— the variable being detailed.
2103	
2104	[[axhrgb_group]]
2105	{axhrgb_group}::
2106	Marks a group boundary within a subdocument.
2107	Lookahead: 1
2108	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2109	— the group being detailed.
2110	The referenced attribute must match an anchor annotated with
2111	{axvr_group}
2112	in the parent document.
2113	
2114	[[axhrgc_gate]]
2115	{axhrgc_gate}::
2116	Marks a conditional activation gate within a group.
2117	Lookahead: 2
2118	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
2119	— the enumerated variable (voicing
2120	{axt_enumeration})
2121	and the specific
2122	{axt_enum_value}
2123	that activates this gate.
2124	The gate variable may belong to a different regime (cross-regime gating is legal).
2125	
2126	[[axhrgv_variable]]
2127	{axhrgv_variable}::
2128	Marks a variable within the current group in a subdocument.
2129	Lookahead: 1
2130	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2131	— the variable being detailed within the current group scope.
2132	
2133	[[axhro_kindle]]
2134	{axhro_kindle}::
2135	Marks a regime kindle operation description in a subdocument.
2136	Lookahead: 1
2137	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2138	— the kit-level kindle operation term (which must be voiced
2139	{axvr_kindle}
2140	in the parent document).
2141	
2142	[[axhro_validate]]
2143	{axhro_validate}::
2144	Marks a regime validate operation description in a subdocument.
2145	Lookahead: 1
2146	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2147	— the kit-level validate operation term (which must be voiced
2148	{axvr_validate}
2149	in the parent document).
2150	
2151	[[axhro_render]]
2152	{axhro_render}::
2153	Marks a regime render operation description in a subdocument.
2154	Lookahead: 1
2155	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2156	— the kit-level render operation term (which must be voiced
2157	{axvr_render}
2158	in the parent document).
2159	
2160	[[axhro_list]]
2161	{axhro_list}::
2162	Marks a regime list operation description in a subdocument.
2163	Lookahead: 1
2164	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2165	— the kit-level list operation term (which must be voiced
2166	{axvr_list}
2167	in the parent document).
2168	Only valid on
2169	{axrd_manifold}
2170	regimes.
2171	
2172	[[axhro_survey]]
2173	{axhro_survey}::
2174	Marks a regime survey operation description in a subdocument.
2175	Lookahead: 1
2176	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2177	— the kit-level survey operation term (which must be voiced
2178	{axvr_survey}
2179	in the parent document).
2180	Only valid on
2181	{axrd_manifold}
2182	regimes.
2183	
2184	[[axhro_audit]]
2185	{axhro_audit}::
2186	Marks a regime audit operation description in a subdocument.
2187	Lookahead: 1
2188	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2189	— the kit-level audit operation term (which must be voiced
2190	{axvr_audit}
2191	in the parent document).
2192	Only valid on
2193	{axrd_manifold}
2194	regimes.
2195	
2196	==== Nesting Rules
2197	
2198	Hierarchy markers establish implicit scope through document order:
2199	
2200	* Higher-level markers implicitly close previous scope (no end markers needed)
2201	* {axhrgb_group},
2202	{axhrgc_gate},
2203	and
2204	{axhrgv_variable}
2205	must appear within
2206	{axhrb_regime}
2207	scope
2208	* {axhrgc_gate}
2209	and
2210	{axhrgv_variable}
2211	must appear within
2212	{axhrgb_group}
2213	scope
2214	* Multiple
2215	{axhrgc_gate}
2216	within one group have AND semantics
2217	* {axhro_kindle},
2218	{axhro_validate},
2219	{axhro_render},
2220	{axhro_list},
2221	{axhro_survey},
2222	and
2223	{axhro_audit}
2224	must appear within
2225	{axhrb_regime}
2226	scope but outside any
2227	{axhrgb_group}
2228	scope
2229	
2230	==== Cross-Document Validation
2231	
2232	* {axhrb_regime}
2233	attribute reference must match a regime defined with
2234	{axvr_regime}
2235	in parent
2236	* {axhrgb_group}
2237	attribute reference must match a group defined with
2238	{axvr_group}
2239	under that regime
2240	* {axhrgc_gate}
2241	first attribute reference must voice
2242	{axt_enumeration};
2243	second must be an
2244	{axt_enum_value}
2245	of that specific enumeration type
2246	
2247	== Axial Lexicon Structural Terms
2248	
2249	[[axl_definition_site]]
2250	{axl_definition_site}::
2251	The location in a parent document where a linked term is defined: anchor, voicing annotation, and definition text.
2252	The
2253	{axl_definition_site}
2254	establishes the term's identity, role, and architectural relationships.
2255	Voicing annotations at the
2256	{axl_definition_site}
2257	may impose positional requirements on linked terms in the definition text that follows.
2258	
2259	[[axl_detail_site]]
2260	{axl_detail_site}::
2261	The location where a linked term's details are elaborated through hierarchy markers and prose.
2262	Typically a subdocument included from the parent, but may be inline within the parent document for compact definitions.
2263	The
2264	{axl_detail_site}
2265	is opened by a hierarchy block marker (e.g.,
2266	{axhob_operation},
2267	{axhrb_regime})
2268	and contains subordinate markers for parameters, steps, variables, or other structural elements.
2269

file.content:
== Axial Dimension Terms

{axd_dimension_s}
are modifiers that appear in
{axl_annotation_s}
to specify characteristics of members and arguments.
Unlike primary
{axl_motif_s},
{axd_dimension_s}
do not stand alone but qualify how a term voices its primary motif.
Applicability of
{axd_dimension_s}
depends on serialization format context.

[[axd_dimension]]
{axd_dimension}::
A characteristic modifier that qualifies a member or argument in an
{axl_annotation}.
{axd_dimension_s}
appear after the primary
{axl_motif}
in annotation format: `//axl_voices primary dimension ...`

=== Value Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
describe value characteristics and apply across most serialization formats.
They encompass both presence semantics (required, optional, defaulted) and structural semantics (repeated, list).

[[axd_required]]
{axd_required}::
Member or argument must be present.
Absence triggers validation error or
{axc_fatal}.

[[axd_optional]]
{axd_optional}::
Member or argument may be omitted.
Absence is valid and handled gracefully.

[[axd_nullable]]
{axd_nullable}::
Null, nil, or None is a valid value, distinct from absent.
Required for JSON where null is an explicit value type.
Some formats (protobuf) conflate nullable with optional.

[[axd_none]]
{axd_none}::
Section or field is explicitly declared as having no content.
Distinguishes intentional absence from omission.
Use in section annotations to mark compliance with required sections that have no applicable content.

[[axd_defaulted]]
{axd_defaulted}::
Has fallback value when absent.
Voicing should specify the default value in prose.

[[axd_repeated]]
{axd_repeated}::
May have multiple values (array, list, repeated field).
Absence typically means empty collection, not error.
Applies to usage sites (arguments, members) where multiple occurrences are permitted.

[[axd_list]]
{axd_list}::
Single value containing delimited multiple values.
Distinct from
{axd_repeated}
which indicates multiple separate occurrences.
Applies to type voicings where the type intrinsically represents a delimited collection.
Voicing should specify the delimiter in prose (space-delimited, comma-delimited, etc.).

=== Format-Specific Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
apply only to specific serialization formats.
Using them in forbidden contexts is a type-scheme error.

[[axd_field_number]]
{axd_field_number}::
Numeric field identifier for protobuf encoding.
Required for protobuf format.
Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.

[[axd_wire_type]]
{axd_wire_type}::
Protobuf encoding type (varint, fixed32, fixed64, length-delimited).
Required for protobuf format.
Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.

[[axd_endianness]]
{axd_endianness}::
Byte order for binary encoding (big-endian, little-endian).
Required for binary format.
Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, protobuf.

[[axd_xml_form]]
{axd_xml_form}::
Whether XML member is attribute or child element.
Required for XML format.
Forbidden for JSON, YAML, protobuf, binary.

[[axd_property]]
{axd_property}::
JSON
{axr_member}
encoded as a named property within its containing
{axr_record_json}.
The wire-level key is a fixed sprue declared at the definition site;
the 1-arity sprue lookahead applies and is fulfilled by a backtick-delimited
{mcm_sprue}
in the following text.
Required for JSON
{axr_member_s}
whose value sits at a named slot.
Forbidden for protobuf, XML, YAML, binary.

[[axd_positional]]
{axd_positional}::
JSON
{axr_member}
encoded by structural position rather than by a named property.
The member's value provides the parent map's key directly;
no separate sprue exists in the wire form.
Suppresses the 1-arity sprue lookahead — under
{axd_positional},
{axr_member}
becomes 0-arity for sprue.
Required for JSON
{axr_member_s}
whose value is the parent map's key (e.g., a record's identity field encoded as the map entry's key).
Forbidden for protobuf, XML, YAML, binary.

Exactly one of
{axd_property}
or
{axd_positional}
must be present on every
{axr_member}
under an
{axr_record_json}
carrier whose value participates in the JSON wire form.
Members that are computed, ephemeral, or stored outside the JSON wire are not
{axr_member_s}
of the
{axr_record_json}
and should not carry the
{axr_member}
voicing in the first place.

[[axd_string]]
{axd_string}::
{axt_enum_value}
encoded as a literal string token in the wire form.
Carries a 1-arity sprue lookahead, fulfilled by a backtick-delimited
{mcm_sprue}
in the following text — the literal wire-level value as it appears in the carrier.
The
{mcm_sprue}
is the exact wire-name; the
{mcm_quoin}
is the conceptual enum-value identity.
Carrier-agnostic: applies wherever an
{axt_enumeration}
serializes its values as string tokens (JSON strings, bash regime values, YAML scalars, env-var values).
Forbidden when the wire form is non-string (numeric, bitmask, object-tagged, positional, untagged variant) — those encodings will mint companion dimensions.

=== Lifecycle Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
qualify
{axo_procedure}
voicings to specify execution lifecycle.
Exactly one lifecycle dimension is required for any
{axo_procedure}
voicing.
Lifecycle dimensions are mutually exclusive.

[[axd_transient]]
{axd_transient}::
{axo_procedure}
executes and completes.
The default lifecycle for most procedures.
{axhoc_completion}
documents exit status or return value.

[[axd_longrunning]]
{axd_longrunning}::
{axo_procedure}
runs continuously until explicitly stopped.
{axhoc_completion}
documents startup success criteria, not termination.
Requires
{axhos_step} (startup)
and
{axhos_step} (shutdown)
sections.
May include
{axhos_step} (health, signals, recovery)
sections.

[[axd_periodic]]
{axd_periodic}::
{axo_procedure}
executes on a schedule.
{axhoc_completion}
applies per-run.
Requires
{axhos_step} (schedule)
section.
May include
{axhos_step} (idempotency, overlap)
sections.

=== Regime Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
qualify regime structure annotations.

[[axd_conditional]]
{axd_conditional}::
Group activation depends on one or more gate conditions.
A group annotated with
{axd_conditional}
requires corresponding hierarchy markers in the subdocument to include at least one
{axhrgc_gate}
specifying the activation criteria.

[[axrd_singleton]]
{axrd_singleton}::
Regime has exactly one assignment source.
No enumeration or cross-instance operations apply.

[[axrd_manifold]]
{axrd_manifold}::
Regime has multiple assignment sources (one per instance).
Enumeration and cross-instance operations are meaningful.

=== Protocol Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
qualify
{axi_protocol}
voicings to specify transport, lifecycle, and concurrency presumptions of the bilateral discipline.

[[axd_file_bus]]
{axd_file_bus}::
Transport dimension on an
{axi_protocol}:
participants communicate via a shared filesystem directory acting as the wire.
The caller writes call envelopes as files; the callee observes atomic file appearance via rename(2) (write-temp-then-rename) and writes return envelopes the same way.
No sockets, no in-process shared memory — POSIX file I/O is the entire wire.
+
*Index discipline:* participants coordinate transaction ordering via monotonic indices assigned by the caller and embedded in filenames or envelope bodies.
Indices are session-local and reset on callee restart; the caller detects restart via index-collision or an explicit readiness exchange.
Filename conventions for call and return envelopes are declared in the voicing spec, not in this motif.
+
*Atomicity:* every envelope write uses the rename(2) idiom so receivers never observe partial content.
+
*Wire-format neutrality:*
{axd_file_bus}
is transport-only; envelope contents are described by separate
{axr_record_s}
voicings (e.g.,
{axr_record_json}).

[[axd_disjoint_lifecycle]]
{axd_disjoint_lifecycle}::
Lifecycle dimension on an
{axi_protocol}:
the caller and callee have independent start and stop lifecycles.
Neither actor can assume the other exists when it itself begins;
each call must handle the possibility that the peer is absent, starting, or no longer the same instance that handled the previous call.
+
Implies the caller must observe callee presence before committing to a transaction, and must gracefully degrade or retry when the callee is unavailable.
The specific observation mechanism (readiness probe, file-presence check, timeout) is declared in the protocol's detail site, not in this motif.
+
Contrasts with shared-session protocols where caller and callee share a bounded lifetime (e.g., an HTTP request-response pair scoped to a single process).

=== Dimension Applicability

Per-format guidance for
{axd_dimension}
usage:

[cols="<,^,^,^,^,^",options="header"]
|===
|Dimension |JSON |Protobuf |XML |Binary |YAML

|{axd_required}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
|{axd_optional}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
|{axd_nullable}     |required  |implicit  |recommend |context   |recommend
|{axd_defaulted}    |optional  |required  |optional  |optional  |optional
|{axd_repeated}     |recommend |required  |recommend |recommend |recommend
|{axd_list}         |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional
|{axd_field_number} |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
|{axd_wire_type}    |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
|{axd_endianness}   |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden
|{axd_xml_form}     |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden
|===

Legend: required = must specify; recommend = should specify; optional = may specify; implicit = format handles; context = depends on usage; forbidden = not applicable.

== Axial Format Terms

{axf_format_s}
specify how configuration data is serialized and carried.
{axrg_assignment_s}
use a particular
{axf_format}
to encode
{axrg_variable}
values in files that can be sourced, included, or parsed.

[[axf_format]]
{axf_format}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a serialization encoding for configuration data.
{axf_format_s}
determine how
{axrg_assignment_s}
are written to and read from files.

[[axf_bash]]
{axf_bash}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing bash script format for configuration.
Files are source-able via `. file` or `source file`.
Variables declared as `VAR=value` without export, or `export VAR=value` for environment propagation.

[[axf_makefile]]
{axf_makefile}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing makefile format for configuration.
Files are includable via `include file`.
Variables declared as `VAR := value` for immediate expansion or `VAR = value` for deferred expansion.

[[axf_json]]
{axf_json}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing JSON format for configuration.
Standard JSON object structure with string keys mapping to typed values.
Parsed by standard JSON libraries.

[[axf_yaml]]
{axf_yaml}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing YAML format for configuration.
Superset of JSON with additional syntax for readability.
Parsed by YAML libraries with attention to version-specific semantics.

== Axial Regime Terms

{axrg_regime_s}
are structured configuration systems that coordinate specification, validation, and rendering of typed configuration data.

[[axrg_regime]]
{axrg_regime}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a structured configuration system.
A
{axrg_regime}
binds related
{axrg_variable_s}
under a namespace prefix, with coordinated specification, validation, and rendering components.
The specification defines what can be configured;
{axrg_assignment_s}
provide specific values.

[[axrg_variable]]
{axrg_variable}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a named configuration element at schema level.
Each
{axrg_variable}
belongs to an
{axrg_regime}
and defines a name, type constraints, and relationships with other variables.
{axrg_variable_s}
are the "what can be configured" definitions;
{axrg_assignment_s}
bind values to them.

[[axrg_assignment]]
{axrg_assignment}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a specific value bound to an
{axrg_variable}.
A single binding — one variable, one value.
{axrg_assignment_s}
are conveyed by an
{axrg_carrier},
whose concrete form and
{axf_format}
depend on the regime's provenance.

[[axrg_carrier]]
{axrg_carrier}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing the artifact holding one instance's complete set of
{axrg_assignment_s}
for a single
{axrg_regime}.
The carrier's concrete form is selected by its provenance
{axd_dimension}:
{axrd_file_sourced}
manifests as a file at a path (encoding via
{axf_format});
{axrd_constructed}
as values assembled by a defining module and sourced into consumers;
{axrd_env_inherited}
as an inherited process environment scope.
A
{axrd_singleton}
regime has exactly one carrier;
a
{axrd_manifold}
regime has one carrier per instance.
Only a
{axrd_file_sourced}
carrier has a path locus: the singleton case resolves to a single path, the manifold case to one templated path per instance.
Whether that locus earns a dedicated quoin or is composed in prose from a root quoin plus a literal leaf is a per-specification judgment, not a property of the motif.
Virtual carriers
({axrd_constructed},
{axrd_env_inherited})
have no consumer-facing path.

[[axrg_prefix]]
{axrg_prefix}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing the stable character sequence that namespaces all
{axrg_variable_s}
within an
{axrg_regime}.
Typically composed of a project marker and regime marker (e.g., `RBRR_` = `RB` + `RR` + `_`).
Once established, an
{axrg_prefix}
becomes deeply embedded across code, configuration files, and documentation.
Changing an
{axrg_prefix}
requires coordinated refactoring across all consuming artifacts.

== Axial Regime Provenance Dimensions

Provenance dimensions qualify an
{axrg_carrier}
to declare its concrete form and how its
{axrg_assignment_s}
arrive at
{axvr_kindle}
time.
They are declared on
{axvr_regime}
to state which provenance(s) a regime's carrier supports.

[[axrd_file_sourced]]
{axrd_file_sourced}::
Assignments loaded from a persistent file via source or equivalent mechanism.
The file path is known at load time and the regime module is responsible for locating and sourcing it.

[[axrd_env_inherited]]
{axrd_env_inherited}::
Assignments received as environment variables from a parent process.
The regime module finds variables already in scope; no file sourcing occurs.

[[axrd_constructed]]
{axrd_constructed}::
Assignments computed or set programmatically before kindle.
Values are assembled by application logic rather than loaded from a static source.

== Axial Regime Annotation Terms

Regime annotations use the prefix-discriminated form (`//ax...` with no space after `//`) to provide structured metadata about regime definitions in AsciiDoc specifications.
Two complementary annotation families enable regime specification across parent documents and their subdocuments.

=== Definition-Site Voicing Annotations (axvr_*)

{axvr_*} annotations appear in parent documents between anchor and definition, declaring the regime role of the term being defined.
The surface syntax follows the prefix-discriminated pattern:

[source,asciidoc]
----
[[rbrv_sigil]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_xname
{rbrv_sigil}::
Vessel identifier for build configurations.
----

The annotation line starts with `//axvr_` (no space after `//`), followed by the specific voicing term, then space-separated dimension and type terms.
The anchor immediately preceding the annotation must match the first
{mcm_attribute_reference}
in the definition text that follows.

[[axvr_regime]]
{axvr_regime}::
Voices a regime definition.
The annotated term defines a structured configuration system that binds related
{axrg_variable_s}
under a namespace prefix.
+
{axd_dimension_s}:
{axf_format}
(specifying how
{axrg_assignment_s}
are carried).

[[axvr_variable]]
{axvr_variable}::
Voices a regime variable definition.
The annotated term defines a named configuration element within a regime.
The definition text must include, as its second
{mcm_attribute_reference},
the parent regime (an anchor that voices
{axrg_regime}).
This ties each variable unequivocally to its regime.
+
{axd_dimension_s}:
{axd_required}
or
{axd_optional},
type
{axl_motif}
(base type or type voicing).

[[axvr_group]]
{axvr_group}::
Voices a regime group definition.
The annotated term defines a named grouping of related
{axrg_variable_s}
within a regime.
The definition text must include, as its second
{mcm_attribute_reference},
the parent regime (an anchor that voices
{axrg_regime}).
+
{axd_dimension_s}:
{axd_conditional}
(group activation depends on gate conditions in subdocument).

[[axvr_kindle]]
{axvr_kindle}::
Voices a regime kindle operation definition.
The annotated term is a dedicated
{mcm_quoin}
naming the silent initialization phase for exactly one
{axrg_regime}:
setting defaults, detecting unexpected variables, and building derived structures.

[[axvr_validate]]
{axvr_validate}::
Voices a regime validate operation definition.
The annotated term is a dedicated
{mcm_quoin}
naming the strict verification phase for exactly one
{axrg_regime}:
checking all type and constraint requirements, failing on first violation.

[[axvr_render]]
{axvr_render}::
Voices a regime render operation definition.
The annotated term is a dedicated
{mcm_quoin}
naming the diagnostic display phase for exactly one
{axrg_regime}:
producing human-readable presentation of current state.
Rendering occurs before validation so all fields are visible on failure.

[[axvr_list]]
{axvr_list}::
Voices a regime list operation definition.
The annotated term is a dedicated
{mcm_quoin}
naming the enumeration operation for exactly one
{axrd_manifold}
{axrg_regime}:
listing available instances.

[[axvr_survey]]
{axvr_survey}::
Voices a regime survey operation definition.
The annotated term is a dedicated
{mcm_quoin}
naming the read-only cross-instance summary operation for exactly one
{axrd_manifold}
{axrg_regime}.

[[axvr_audit]]
{axvr_audit}::
Voices a regime audit operation definition.
The annotated term is a dedicated
{mcm_quoin}
naming the cross-instance validation plus external verification operation for exactly one
{axrd_manifold}
{axrg_regime}.

=== Standalone Hierarchy Markers (axhr*_)

Hierarchy markers appear in regime subdocuments as standalone annotations, NOT between anchor and definition.
Each marker uses the prefix-discriminated form and reads ahead a specified number of
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
from the following text to establish hierarchical context.

The surface syntax:

[source,asciidoc]
----
//axhrb_regime
{rbrv_regime}

//axhrgv_variable
{rbrv_sigil}
----

The hierarchy marker line starts with `//axhr` (no space after `//`), followed by the sub-prefix identifying the marker type.
Following text contains the
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
that the marker reads ahead.

==== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities

[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
|===
|Marker |Lookahead |Reads

|{axhrb_regime}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The regime this subdoc describes
|{axhrv_variable}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |An ungrouped regime variable
|{axhrgb_group}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A group (must match {axvr_group} in parent)
|{axhrgc_gate}      |2 {mcm_attribute_reference_s}|Enumerated variable + enum value
|{axhrgv_variable}  |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A variable within current group
|{axhro_kindle}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level kindle operation for this regime
|{axhro_validate}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level validate operation for this regime
|{axhro_render}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level render operation for this regime
|{axhro_list}       |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level list operation for this regime
|{axhro_survey}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level survey operation for this regime
|{axhro_audit}      |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level audit operation for this regime
|===

[[axhrb_regime]]
{axhrb_regime}::
Marks the regime that a subdocument describes.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the regime this subdoc describes.
The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
{axvr_regime}
in the parent document.

[[axhrv_variable]]
{axhrv_variable}::
Marks an ungrouped regime variable in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the variable being detailed.

[[axhrgb_group]]
{axhrgb_group}::
Marks a group boundary within a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the group being detailed.
The referenced attribute must match an anchor annotated with
{axvr_group}
in the parent document.

[[axhrgc_gate]]
{axhrgc_gate}::
